uses.description: Returns 1 if the result of the sub-formula is strictly less than the threshold, otherwise 0. Useful as a conditional check in formulas where a boolean outcome is needed based on whether a computed value falls below a fixed value.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Numeric value to compare against. Parsed as a float literal.

arguments.name: formula
arguments.description: Sub-formula whose computed result is compared against the threshold. Consumes the remainder of the operand string after the threshold.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the result of the sub-formula is strictly greater than the threshold, otherwise 0. Used to gate effects or trigger behavior when a computed value rises above a specified value.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Numeric value to compare against. Parsed as a float literal.

arguments.name: formula
arguments.description: Sub-formula whose computed result is compared against the threshold. Consumes the remainder of the operand string after the threshold.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the result of the sub-formula equals the threshold exactly, otherwise 0. Used to check for precise numeric equality between a fixed value and a computed value.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Numeric value to compare against for equality. Parsed as a float literal.

arguments.name: formula
arguments.description: Sub-formula whose computed result is checked for equality against the value. Consumes the remainder of the operand string after the value.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the result of the sub-formula does not equal the threshold, otherwise 0. Used to check for inequality between a fixed value and a computed value.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Numeric value to compare against for inequality. Parsed as a float literal.

arguments.name: formula
arguments.description: Sub-formula whose computed result is checked for non-equality against the value. Consumes the remainder of the operand string after the value.

uses.description: Returns the larger of the threshold and the sub-formula's result, effectively clamping the computed value to be at least the threshold (a floor). Despite the name, this enforces a minimum value.

arguments.name: floor
arguments.description: The minimum value the result is allowed to be. Parsed as a float literal.

arguments.name: formula
arguments.description: Sub-formula whose computed result is clamped to be at least the floor. Consumes the remainder of the operand string after the floor.

uses.description: Returns the smaller of the threshold and the sub-formula's result, effectively clamping the computed value to be at most the threshold (a ceiling). Despite the name, this enforces a maximum value.

arguments.name: ceiling
arguments.description: The maximum value the result is allowed to be. Parsed as a float literal.

arguments.name: formula
arguments.description: Sub-formula whose computed result is clamped to be at most the ceiling. Consumes the remainder of the operand string after the ceiling.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the sub-formula's result is strictly between the two bounds (exclusive on both ends), otherwise 0. The order of the bounds does not matter; the smaller one is treated as the lower bound and the larger one as the upper bound.

arguments.name: bound1
arguments.description: First bound for the range check. Can be either the lower or upper bound; the operator orders them automatically.

arguments.name: bound2
arguments.description: Second bound for the range check. Can be either the lower or upper bound; the operator orders them automatically.

arguments.name: formula
arguments.description: Sub-formula whose computed result is tested against the range. Consumes the remainder of the operand string after both bounds.

uses.description: Returns the absolute value of the sub-formula's result. Negative values become positive; positive values and zero are unchanged.

arguments.name: formula
arguments.description: Sub-formula whose computed result will have its sign stripped. Consumes the remainder of the operand string after the operator name.

uses.description: Returns the sub-formula's result rounded down to the nearest integer. For example, 3.7 becomes 3, and -1.2 becomes -2.

arguments.name: formula
arguments.description: Sub-formula whose computed result will be rounded down. Consumes the remainder of the operand string after the operator name.

uses.description: Returns the sub-formula's result rounded up to the nearest integer. For example, 3.2 becomes 4, and -1.7 becomes -1.

arguments.name: formula
arguments.description: Sub-formula whose computed result will be rounded up. Consumes the remainder of the operand string after the operator name.

uses.description: Returns the sub-formula's result rounded to the nearest integer (banker's rounding for .5 values, as per .NET's Math.Round default).

arguments.name: formula
arguments.description: Sub-formula whose computed result will be rounded. Consumes the remainder of the operand string after the operator name.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the sub-formula's result is less than or equal to zero, otherwise 0. Inverts truthiness so 0 (or any negative value) becomes 1 and positive values become 0.

arguments.name: formula
arguments.description: Sub-formula whose computed result is inverted to a boolean. Consumes the remainder of the operand string after the operator name.

uses.description: Evaluates the sub-formula and returns its result unchanged, while also drawing a debug overlay showing the formula string and its computed value on screen. Useful for inspecting intermediate values during development.

arguments.name: formula
arguments.description: Sub-formula to evaluate. The result is both returned and displayed as a debug overlay. Consumes the remainder of the operand string after the operator name.

uses.description: Evaluates the sub-formula using a custom target tile coordinate instead of the original one. The X and Y arguments form a new TileCoord that replaces targetTC during the inner evaluation, allowing queries about a specific map location rather than the action's actual target tile.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: The X coordinate of the temporary target tile. Parsed as an integer.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: The Y coordinate of the temporary target tile. Parsed as an integer.

arguments.name: formula
arguments.description: Sub-formula to evaluate with the overridden target tile coordinate. Consumes the remainder of the operand string after the X and Y values.

uses.description: Evaluates the sub-formula with the caster and target swapped, so that operators that normally read from the caster will instead read from the target and vice versa. Useful for reciprocal calculations such as evaluating the target's perspective.

arguments.name: formula
arguments.description: Sub-formula to evaluate with caster and target swapped. Consumes the remainder of the operand string after the operator name.

uses.description: Evaluates the sub-formula using a specific actor (looked up by ID in the current zone) as the caster instead of the original caster. Useful for querying values from a known actor regardless of who is actually performing the action.

arguments.name: actorId
arguments.description: Identifier of the actor in the current zone to substitute as caster. Looked up via ZoneManager.

arguments.name: formula
arguments.description: Sub-formula to evaluate with the substituted caster. Consumes the remainder of the operand string after the actor ID.

uses.description: Evaluates the sub-formula using a specific actor (looked up by ID in the current zone) as the target instead of the original target. Useful for querying values about a known actor regardless of who is actually being targeted.

arguments.name: actorId
arguments.description: Identifier of the actor in the current zone to substitute as target. Looked up via ZoneManager.

arguments.name: formula
arguments.description: Sub-formula to evaluate with the substituted target. Consumes the remainder of the operand string after the actor ID.

uses.description: Returns the named actor value of the caster, including any active buffs and effects. The special name 'rank' returns the rank parameter passed into the formula instead of an actor value.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the actor value to look up on the caster (such as HP, MP, STR). The special string 'rank' returns the rank parameter. If the name is not a known actor value, the operator falls back to reading a field of the same name from the caster via reflection.

uses.description: Returns the base value of a named actor value on the caster, ignoring any active buffs, debuffs, or temporary modifiers. The special name 'rank' returns the rank parameter passed into the formula instead of an actor value.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the actor value to look up on the caster, fetched as a base value with no modifiers applied. The special string 'rank' returns the rank parameter.

uses.description: Returns the named actor value of the target, including any active buffs and effects. If the named value is not a known actor value, the operator falls back to reading a field of the same name from the target via reflection.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the actor value to look up on the target (such as HP, MP, DEF). If the name is not a known actor value, the operator falls back to reading a field of the same name from the target via reflection.

uses.description: Returns the base value of a named actor value on the target, ignoring any active buffs, debuffs, or temporary modifiers. The special name 'rank' returns the rank parameter passed into the formula instead of an actor value.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the actor value to look up on the target, fetched as a base value with no modifiers applied. The special string 'rank' returns the rank parameter.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named actor value on the caster equals exactly 1, otherwise 0. Convenient shorthand for checking a boolean-style actor value (where 1 means true).

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the actor value on the caster to test for equality with 1.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named actor value on the caster equals the specified value exactly, otherwise 0. Used for equality checks against arbitrary numeric values.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the actor value on the caster to compare.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Numeric value to compare the actor value against for equality.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named actor value on the target equals the specified value exactly, otherwise 0. Used for equality checks against arbitrary numeric values.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the actor value on the target to compare.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Numeric value to compare the actor value against for equality.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named actor value on the caster is strictly greater than the specified value, otherwise 0. Used to gate behavior when a caster stat exceeds a threshold.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the actor value on the caster to compare.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Numeric threshold to compare the actor value against.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named actor value on the target is strictly greater than the specified value, otherwise 0. Used to gate behavior when a target stat exceeds a threshold.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the actor value on the target to compare.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Numeric threshold to compare the actor value against.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named actor value on the caster is strictly less than the specified value, otherwise 0. Used to gate behavior when a caster stat falls below a threshold.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the actor value on the caster to compare.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Numeric threshold to compare the actor value against.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named actor value on the target is strictly less than the specified value, otherwise 0. Used to gate behavior when a target stat falls below a threshold.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the actor value on the target to compare.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Numeric threshold to compare the actor value against.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named actor value on the target equals exactly 1, otherwise 0. Convenient shorthand for checking a boolean-style actor value (where 1 means true).

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the actor value on the target to test for equality with 1.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named actor value on the target equals exactly 99, otherwise 0. Specialized check often used for cap-related logic where 99 represents a maxed stat.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the actor value on the target to test for equality with 99.

uses.description: Returns the larger of the caster's and the target's value for the named actor value. Useful when an effect should scale with the higher of two actor stats.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the actor value to look up on both caster and target; the larger of the two values is returned.

uses.description: Returns the remaining duration of an active actor value effect (buff/debuff) on the caster. Used to query how long a status will last on the caster.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the actor value effect on the caster whose remaining duration is queried.

uses.description: Returns the remaining duration of an active actor value effect (buff/debuff) on the target. Used to query how long a status will last on the target.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the actor value effect on the target whose remaining duration is queried.

uses.description: Returns the portion of the caster's named actor value that comes from a specific source (such as a particular item, ability, or buff). Useful for isolating one contributing source's effect on a total stat.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the actor value to look up on the caster.

arguments.name: source
arguments.description: Identifier of the source whose contribution to the actor value is queried.

uses.description: Returns the portion of the target's named actor value that comes from a specific source (such as a particular item, ability, or buff). Useful for isolating one contributing source's effect on a total stat.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the actor value to look up on the target.

arguments.name: source
arguments.description: Identifier of the source whose contribution to the actor value is queried.

uses.description: Returns the caster's named actor value minus contributions from a specific source. Useful for computing a stat as if a particular item, ability, or buff weren't applied.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the actor value to look up on the caster.

arguments.name: source
arguments.description: Identifier of the source whose contribution should be excluded from the actor value.

uses.description: Returns the target's named actor value minus contributions from a specific source. Useful for computing a stat as if a particular item, ability, or buff weren't applied.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the actor value to look up on the target.

arguments.name: source
arguments.description: Identifier of the source whose contribution should be excluded from the actor value.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the caster has at least one active actor value effect of the given name where the magnitude (plus any formula modifier) is zero or negative and the duration is a normal timed value (not -1, -2, or 0). Used to detect that a stat is being suppressed or weakened by some active effect.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the actor value effect on the caster to check for reducing/suppressing instances.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the target has at least one active actor value effect of the given name where the magnitude (plus any formula modifier) is zero or negative and the duration is a normal timed value (not -1, -2, or 0). Used to detect that a stat is being suppressed or weakened by some active effect.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the actor value effect on the target to check for reducing/suppressing instances.

uses.description: Returns a named parameter of the caster's currently active weapon (the main hand by default, or the offhand if the formula is being evaluated with offhand parameters in effect). The parameter can be a property like damage, range, or another formula-driven stat associated with the weapon.

arguments.name: parameter
arguments.description: Name of the weapon parameter to look up (such as damage, range, maxRange, weight, throwDamageMod, throwRangePenalty, or any other named formula on the weapon).

uses.description: Returns a named parameter of the caster's opposite-hand weapon: the offhand when the formula is evaluated with main-hand parameters, or the main hand when offhand parameters are in effect. Used to read stats from whichever weapon is not currently being treated as primary.

arguments.name: parameter
arguments.description: Name of the weapon parameter to look up on the opposite-hand weapon (such as damage, range, weight, or any other named formula on the weapon).

uses.description: Returns 1 if the caster has the specified actor class as any of their four class slots (primary, secondary, third, or fourth class), otherwise 0. Used to gate effects on whether the caster belongs to a particular class.

arguments.name: classId
arguments.description: Identifier of the actor class to check against the caster's four class slots.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the target's combat team identifier matches the specified team string, otherwise 0. Used to detect whether the target belongs to a particular combat team (such as 'player', 'enemy', or any custom team name).

arguments.name: team
arguments.description: Combat team identifier to compare against the target's combat team.

uses.description: Returns the X tile coordinate of the actor with the specified ID in the current zone. Returns 0 if no such actor exists.

arguments.name: actorId
arguments.description: Identifier of the actor whose X tile position is queried.

uses.description: Returns the Y tile coordinate of the actor with the specified ID in the current zone. Returns 0 if no such actor exists.

arguments.name: actorId
arguments.description: Identifier of the actor whose Y tile position is queried.

uses.description: Returns the tile distance between two named actors in the current zone. Returns 0 if either actor cannot be found.

arguments.name: actorId1
arguments.description: Identifier of the first actor.

arguments.name: actorId2
arguments.description: Identifier of the second actor. Distance is measured in tiles between the two actors' positions.

uses.description: Counts how many pending action timers are currently targeting the target tile. Optionally filters to only count timers for a specific action ID and also adds the count of already-queued executions of that action.

arguments.name: actionId
arguments.description: Optional action identifier to restrict counting to a specific action. If omitted, all action timers targeting the tile are counted.

uses.description: Counts how many pending action timers are targeting the target tile, plus the count of already-queued executions and tile-affecter timers for the specified action. Behaves like numActionsTargetingTile but additionally includes tile-affecter timers in the count.

arguments.name: actionId
arguments.description: Action identifier used both to restrict the timer count and to look up additional queued executions and tile-affecter timers.

uses.description: Counts how many non-incapacitated allies of the caster are standing within the named area of effect centered on the target's tile. Allies are determined by sharing the caster's hostile flag.

arguments.name: aoeId
arguments.description: Identifier of an AreaOfEffect data entry that defines the region around the target's tile to search for allies.

uses.description: Counts how many party actors are within a given tile distance of the specified XY coordinate. Default distance is 2 tiles if no distance argument is supplied.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: X coordinate of the location to measure distance from.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: Y coordinate of the location to measure distance from.

arguments.name: distance
arguments.description: Maximum tile distance to consider a party actor as 'near' the XY coordinate. Defaults to 2 if not provided.

uses.description: Counts actors within the named area of effect centered on the caster (oriented by the caster's facing). If no actor value argument is provided, returns the total count of actors in the AoE; otherwise sums the specified actor value across those actors.

arguments.name: aoeId
arguments.description: Identifier of an AreaOfEffect data entry that defines the region around the caster.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Optional actor value name. If provided, the operator sums this value across all actors in the AoE rather than counting them.

uses.description: Counts actors within the named area of effect centered on the target (or on targetTC if no target actor is given). If no actor value argument is provided, returns the total count of actors in the AoE; otherwise sums the specified actor value across those actors.

arguments.name: aoeId
arguments.description: Identifier of an AreaOfEffect data entry that defines the region around the target.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Optional actor value name. If provided, the operator sums this value across all actors in the AoE rather than counting them.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the player is currently in dialog with the actor whose ID matches the argument, otherwise 0. Used to gate logic on the identity of the current conversation partner.

arguments.name: actorId
arguments.description: Identifier of the actor to check against the currently-talking-to actor.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named actor in the current zone is able to act this turn, otherwise 0. Considers global zone restrictions (cantAct flag, peaceful zones with cannotActInPeacefulZones items, tiles with cannotAct items) in addition to the actor's own permission state.

arguments.name: actorId
arguments.description: Identifier of the actor whose ability to act is queried.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named actor in the current zone is currently allowed to move, otherwise 0. Checks the input manager's permission state for movement.

arguments.name: actorId
arguments.description: Identifier of the actor whose ability to move is queried.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named actor in the current zone is currently allowed to manipulate items, otherwise 0. Despite the name, the check uses the same permission flags as movement.

arguments.name: actorId
arguments.description: Identifier of the actor whose ability to manipulate items is queried.

uses.description: Returns the count of items of the given type either in the target actor's inventory (if a target actor is set) or stacked on the target tile (if only a target tile is set). Total quantity rather than number of distinct stacks.

arguments.name: itemTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of the item type to count on the target actor or tile.

uses.description: Returns the count of items of the given type located within a tile radius of the target (or targetTC if no target actor is set). The radius is measured in tiles.

arguments.name: itemTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of the item type to count.

arguments.name: radius
arguments.description: Maximum tile distance from the target within which to count items. Parsed as an integer.

uses.description: Returns the count of items with the named ItemType special property located within a tile radius of the target (or targetTC if no target actor is set). Useful for finding all items of a category (such as torches or magical items) regardless of type.

arguments.name: specialProperty
arguments.description: Name of an ItemType special property; items with this flag will be counted.

arguments.name: radius
arguments.description: Maximum tile distance from the target within which to count items. Parsed as an integer.

uses.description: Returns the total quantity of items emitting light located within a tile radius of the target (or targetTC if no target actor is set). The search ignores line-of-sight and line-of-effect and counts every item whose ItemType has at least one light entry.

arguments.name: radius
arguments.description: Maximum tile distance from the target within which to find light-source items. Parsed as an integer.

uses.description: Returns the total quantity of items belonging to the specified ItemCategory located within a tile radius of the target (or targetTC if no target actor is set). Useful for counting all weapons, armor, foods, etc. regardless of specific type.

arguments.name: itemCategory
arguments.description: Name of an ItemType.ItemCategory enum value to filter counted items.

arguments.name: radius
arguments.description: Maximum tile distance from the target within which to count items. Parsed as an integer.

uses.description: Sums the quantities of items located in tiles within the named area of effect centered on the caster's tile. Returns 0 if there is no caster.

arguments.name: aoeId
arguments.description: Identifier of an AreaOfEffect data entry that defines the region around the caster.

arguments.name: field
arguments.description: Optional name of an ItemType field. When provided alongside the value argument, only items whose field equals the specified value are counted.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Optional sanitized string value that the named field must equal for the item to be counted. Required when the field argument is provided.

uses.description: Sums the quantities of items located in tiles within the named area of effect centered on the target (or on targetTC if no target actor is given). Useful for measuring loot density or counting specific items near a location.

arguments.name: aoeId
arguments.description: Identifier of an AreaOfEffect data entry that defines the region around the target.

arguments.name: field
arguments.description: Optional name of an ItemType field. When provided alongside the value argument, only items whose field equals the specified value are counted.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Optional sanitized string value that the named field must equal for the item to be counted. Required when the field argument is provided.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the target actor (or target tile) holds strictly more than the specified count of an item type, otherwise 0. Used to gate logic on whether an entity has more than a certain quantity of an item.

arguments.name: itemTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of the item type to count on the target actor or tile.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Count to compare against; the operator returns 1 only if the actual count is strictly greater.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the total number of items of the given type anywhere in the current zone is strictly more than the threshold, otherwise 0. Used to gate logic on global item presence in the zone.

arguments.name: itemTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of the item type to count across the entire zone.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Count to compare against; the operator returns 1 only if the actual zone-wide count is strictly greater.

uses.description: With three arguments (x, y, itemTypeId), returns the count of items of that type at the given tile coordinate. With only an itemTypeId argument, returns 1 if the target tile contains at least one such item, otherwise 0.

arguments.name: x_or_itemTypeId
arguments.description: Either the X coordinate (when used with y and itemTypeId) or the item type identifier (when used in the targetTC-based two-argument form). Parsed as an integer if used as a coordinate.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: Y coordinate of the tile to check. Required when the three-argument coordinate form is used.

arguments.name: itemTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of the item type to count at the coordinate. Required when the three-argument coordinate form is used.

uses.description: Returns the total count of items of the given type anywhere in the current zone (including all tiles and actor inventories the zone tracks).

arguments.name: itemTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of the item type to count across the entire zone.

uses.description: Returns the total count of items with the named ItemType special property anywhere in the current zone. Useful for counting all items of a category (such as light sources) regardless of specific type.

arguments.name: specialProperty
arguments.description: Name of an ItemType special property; items with this flag are counted.

uses.description: Returns the combined count of items of the given type that exist anywhere in the current zone plus those carried by the player party. Equivalent to evaluating 'itemsZone:X + partyItem:X'.

arguments.name: itemTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of the item type to count across both the zone and the party's combined inventories.

uses.description: Returns the count of items with the named ItemType special property in the target actor's inventory (including contained items). Falls back to counting non-buried items with that property on the targetTC tile if no target actor is set.

arguments.name: specialProperty
arguments.description: Name of an ItemType special property to filter inventory items by.

uses.description: Returns the count of non-buried items with the named ItemType special property on the target tile (targetTC). Unlike itemsAtTargetSpecialProp, this never inspects an actor's inventory.

arguments.name: specialProperty
arguments.description: Name of an ItemType special property to filter tile items by.

uses.description: Sums the quantities of items on the target tile (targetTC) whose ItemType has at least one elemental reaction triggering the named action. Useful for detecting how many reactive items are present at a location.

arguments.name: actionId
arguments.description: Identifier of the action whose reaction should be matched against each item's reactions list.

uses.description: Returns the count of items of the given type on the tile occupied by the target actor (or on targetTC if no actor target is set). Counts only items physically present on the tile, not items inside an actor's inventory.

arguments.name: itemTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of the item type to count on the target tile.

uses.description: Returns the count of items of the given type on the tile occupied by the caster. Counts only items physically present on the tile, not items inside the caster's inventory.

arguments.name: itemTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of the item type to count on the caster's tile.

uses.description: Returns 1 if a single new item of the named type can be placed on the target tile (targetTC), otherwise 0. Useful for checking whether a tile has space for an item drop.

arguments.name: itemTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of the item type whose placement is being checked.

uses.description: Returns the configured value (sale/base price) of the specified item type from the data tables. Returns 0 if the item type does not exist.

arguments.name: itemTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of an item type in the data tables whose value is queried.

uses.description: Returns the configured value of the specified actor type from the data tables (often used for ship types and their resale/build value). Returns 0 if the actor type does not exist.

arguments.name: actorTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of an actor type in the data tables whose value is queried.

uses.description: Returns the current buy price of the specified item type, including any dynamic price modifiers applied by the data system. Returns 0 if the item type does not exist.

arguments.name: itemTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of an item type in the data tables whose buy price is queried.

uses.description: Returns the total quantity of the specified item type held across the entire player party — counting party-actor inventories, the rest of the player fleet's crew, the player's stash, and items currently held by the cursor. Useful for checking party-wide ownership of a resource.

arguments.name: itemTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of the item type to count across all party-accessible inventories.

uses.description: Returns the total quantity of items with the named ItemType special property held across the entire player party — counting party-actor inventories, the rest of the player fleet's crew, the player's stash, and items currently held by the cursor.

arguments.name: specialProperty
arguments.description: Name of an ItemType special property; items with this flag are counted everywhere accessible to the party.

uses.description: Returns the total quantity of an item type — identified indirectly through a global variable storing the item ID — held across the player party (party inventories, the rest of the player fleet's crew, stash, and held cursor items). If a threshold argument is provided, returns 1 if the total meets or exceeds the threshold and 0 otherwise.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a string global variable; its current value is treated as an item type identifier.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Optional threshold. When provided, the operator returns 1 if the counted total is greater than or equal to this value, otherwise 0.

uses.description: Returns the quantity of an item type held in the player's cargo inventory. If a threshold argument is provided instead, returns 1 if the cargo count meets or exceeds the threshold and 0 otherwise.

arguments.name: itemTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of the item type to count in the cargo inventory.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Optional threshold. When provided, the operator returns 1 if the cargo count is greater than or equal to this value, otherwise 0.

uses.description: Returns the quantity of items with the named ItemType special property in the player's cargo inventory. If a threshold argument is provided, returns 1 if the count meets or exceeds the threshold and 0 otherwise.

arguments.name: specialProperty
arguments.description: Name of an ItemType special property; items with this flag are counted in cargo.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Optional threshold. When provided, the operator returns 1 if the cargo count is greater than or equal to this value, otherwise 0.

uses.description: Returns the quantity of an item type — identified indirectly through a global variable storing the item ID — in the player's cargo inventory. If a threshold argument is provided, returns 1 if the count meets or exceeds the threshold and 0 otherwise.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a string global variable; its current value is treated as an item type identifier.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Optional threshold. When provided, the operator returns 1 if the cargo count is greater than or equal to this value, otherwise 0.

uses.description: Returns 1 if every item ID referenced in the named item type's 'toMake' list has a positive value in its corresponding global variable (i.e., every required ingredient has been crafted/spawned at least once), otherwise 0. Used by recipe completion or crafting goal checks.

arguments.name: itemTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of an item type whose 'toMake' list of ingredient IDs is iterated. Each ingredient global variable must be ≥ 1 for the operator to return 1.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named item type has the specified property matching the given value, otherwise 0. Useful for checking arbitrary fields, list membership, or enum flags on an ItemType data entry.

arguments.name: itemTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of an item type in the data tables.

arguments.name: property
arguments.description: Name of a property/field on the ItemType to check.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Value to compare the property against; supports equality, list-contains, and enum flag checks via DataManager.

uses.description: Returns the numeric value of a named field on the specified item type, parsed as a float. Useful for reading numeric properties on ItemType data.

arguments.name: itemTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of an item type in the data tables.

arguments.name: property
arguments.description: Name of a numeric field on the ItemType to look up and parse as a float.

uses.description: Returns 1 if any item currently held by the cursor (the mouse-held items inventory) is of an ItemType whose property matches the specified value, otherwise 0. Useful for input-driven checks on what the player is dragging.

arguments.name: property
arguments.description: Name of a property/field on the ItemType to check.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Value to compare the property against; supports equality, list-contains, and enum flag checks via DataManager.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the caster has any equipped item (in weapon slots 0–1, armor slot 2, or accessory slots 3–4) whose ItemType belongs to the specified exclusive-equipment category, otherwise 0. Used to detect that the caster is wearing/wielding something from a mutually-exclusive equipment group.

arguments.name: category
arguments.description: Sanitized name of an exclusive-equipment category to match against equipped items.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the target has any equipped item (in weapon slots 0–1, armor slot 2, or accessory slots 3–4) whose ItemType belongs to the specified exclusive-equipment category, otherwise 0. Used to detect that the target is wearing/wielding something from a mutually-exclusive equipment group.

arguments.name: category
arguments.description: Sanitized name of an exclusive-equipment category to match against equipped items.

uses.description: Returns 1 if an actor with the specified ID exists in the current zone, otherwise 0. Useful for checking whether a named NPC or party member is present in the loaded zone.

arguments.name: actorId
arguments.description: Identifier of the actor to look up in the current zone.

uses.description: Returns 1 if an actor with the specified ID exists in the current zone and is positioned at the given XY tile coordinate, otherwise 0. Used to check that a specific named actor is at a specific location.

arguments.name: actorId
arguments.description: Identifier of the actor to look up in the current zone.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: X coordinate that the actor must be at. Parsed as an integer.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: Y coordinate that the actor must be at. Parsed as an integer.

uses.description: Counts how many actors in the current zone are of the specified actor type, including actors queued to be added this turn. Useful for spawning logic or detecting enemy populations.

arguments.name: actorTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of the actor type to match against each actor's actorTypeID.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the actor identified by the given ID is the same actor as the current target, otherwise 0. Useful for testing whether a specific actor happens to be the action's target.

arguments.name: actorId
arguments.description: Identifier of an actor to compare against the current target.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the target actor is currently standing on the specified tile coordinate, otherwise 0. Used to gate effects on whether the target occupies a particular tile.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: X coordinate to check against the target's position. Parsed as an integer.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: Y coordinate to check against the target's position. Parsed as an integer.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the target's faction identifier matches the specified faction string exactly, otherwise 0. Used to gate effects on whether the target belongs to a particular faction.

arguments.name: factionId
arguments.description: Faction identifier to compare against the target's faction.

uses.description: Sums the named actor value across every actor currently present in the current zone. Useful for global statistical queries like total HP of all combatants.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the actor value to read from each actor in the zone and sum together.

uses.description: Sums the named actor value across every actor currently participating in combat (i.e., every actor with a scheduled act-timer). Returns 0 if combat mode is not active.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the actor value to read from each combat participant and sum together.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the caster's actor type identifier matches the specified value, otherwise 0. Used to gate behavior on whether the caster is a particular kind of creature.

arguments.name: actorTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of an actor type to compare against the caster's actorTypeID.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the target's actor type identifier matches the specified value, otherwise 0. Used to gate behavior on whether the target is a particular kind of creature.

arguments.name: actorTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of an actor type to compare against the target's actorTypeID.

uses.description: Returns the player's recorded number of kills against the specified actor type, tracked persistently in the game state. Returns 0 if the actor type has never been killed.

arguments.name: actorTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of an actor type whose total recorded kill count is queried.

uses.description: Returns the player's recorded number of kills against an actor type identified indirectly through a global variable. The global variable's string value is used as the actor type ID.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a string global variable; its current value is treated as an actor type identifier whose kill count is returned.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the caster has the named support ability learned, otherwise 0. Used to gate effects on whether the caster possesses a particular passive/support skill.

arguments.name: supportAbilityId
arguments.description: Identifier of a support ability to check on the caster's support abilities list.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the caster does NOT have the named support ability learned, otherwise 0. The negated form of the support/sa/csa check.

arguments.name: supportAbilityId
arguments.description: Identifier of a support ability to check for absence on the caster.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the target has the named support ability learned, otherwise 0. Target-side equivalent of the support/sa/csa check.

arguments.name: supportAbilityId
arguments.description: Identifier of a support ability to check on the target's support abilities list.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the target does NOT have the named support ability learned, otherwise 0. Target-side negated form of the tsa check.

arguments.name: supportAbilityId
arguments.description: Identifier of a support ability to check for absence on the target.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named actor type has the specified property matching the given value, otherwise 0. Useful for checking arbitrary fields, list membership, or enum flags on an actorType data entry.

arguments.name: actorTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of an actor type in the data tables.

arguments.name: property
arguments.description: Name of a property/field on the actorType to check.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Value to compare the property against; supports equality, list-contains, and enum flag checks via DataManager.

uses.description: Returns the numeric value of a named field on the specified actor type, parsed as a float. Useful for reading numeric properties on actorType data entries.

arguments.name: actorTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of an actor type in the data tables.

arguments.name: property
arguments.description: Name of a numeric field on the actorType to look up and parse as a float.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the specified actor ID corresponds to an actor known to the fleet manager (i.e., is a member of a tracked fleet), otherwise 0. Useful for checking whether a named actor is currently part of a fleet.

arguments.name: actorId
arguments.description: Identifier of the actor to look up in the fleet manager.

uses.description: Returns the crew count of the ship-actor with the specified ID in the current zone. Includes whatever the actor's getHowManyCrew method reports (typically combining party-aboard counts and crew tracking).

arguments.name: actorId
arguments.description: Identifier of a ship actor in the current zone whose crew count is queried.

uses.description: Returns the exact number of crew actors associated with the ship-actor of the specified ID in the current zone, reading the raw count from the actor's crew list. Returns 0 if the actor or its crew list does not exist.

arguments.name: actorId
arguments.description: Identifier of a ship actor in the current zone whose raw crew-list count is queried.

uses.description: Returns the number of items (item-slots) in the cargo of the specified fleet. Returns 0 if the fleet has no cargo or does not exist.

arguments.name: fleetId
arguments.description: Identifier of the fleet whose cargo inventory item count is queried.

uses.description: Sums the quantities of an item type in the cargo of a fleet — either the fleet that the target belongs to, or the fleet specified by the optional second argument. Returns 0 if no fleet is found or the item type is unknown.

arguments.name: itemTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of the item type to count in the fleet's cargo.

arguments.name: fleetId
arguments.description: Optional fleet identifier. If omitted, the target actor's fleet is used.

uses.description: Returns the number of boats currently part of the specified fleet (using getBoats which may filter out destroyed/inactive vessels). Returns 0 if the fleet does not exist.

arguments.name: fleetId
arguments.description: Identifier of the fleet whose boat count is queried.

uses.description: Returns the raw number of boats in the specified fleet (counting every entry in the boats list, including any that getBoats might filter). Returns 0 if the fleet does not exist.

arguments.name: fleetId
arguments.description: Identifier of the fleet whose raw boat list count is queried.

uses.description: Returns 1 if any boat in the specified fleet (or the target's fleet, if no fleet ID is provided) is of the given actor type, otherwise 0. Useful for detecting whether a particular ship class is present in a fleet.

arguments.name: actorTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of the ship actor type to look for among the fleet's boats.

arguments.name: fleetId
arguments.description: Optional fleet identifier. If omitted, the target actor's fleet is used.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the specified fleet is marked as elite, otherwise 0. Used to gate logic on whether a fleet is in the elevated 'elite' tier.

arguments.name: fleetId
arguments.description: Identifier of the fleet whose elite flag is checked.

uses.description: Returns a named crew-actor-value summed across the crew of the caster's ship (only when the caster is itself a ship actor). Used by ship abilities that depend on aggregated crew stats.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the crew actor value to aggregate (summed via getCrewActorValue).

uses.description: Returns a named crew-actor-value summed across the crew of the target's ship (only when the target is itself a ship actor). Used by ship abilities that depend on aggregated crew stats.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the crew actor value to aggregate (summed via getCrewActorValue).

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named actor class exists, is marked as selectable, and is not a 'dummy' placeholder class, otherwise 0. Used to filter out internal/hidden classes when offering class choices.

arguments.name: classId
arguments.description: Identifier of an actor class to check for selectability.

uses.description: Without the stat argument, returns the number of relevant stats listed on the named actor class. With both arguments, returns 1 if the named class lists the given stat as a relevant stat and 0 otherwise.

arguments.name: classId
arguments.description: Identifier of an actor class whose relevant stats list is inspected.

arguments.name: stat
arguments.description: Optional stat name. When provided, returns 1 if the class includes this stat in its relevant stats; when omitted, the operator returns the total number of relevant stats on the class.

uses.description: Without the action argument, returns the number of actions listed on the named actor class. With both arguments, returns 1 if the named class teaches the given action and 0 otherwise.

arguments.name: classId
arguments.description: Identifier of an actor class whose actions list is inspected.

arguments.name: actionId
arguments.description: Optional action identifier. When provided, returns 1 if the class teaches this action; when omitted, the operator returns the total number of actions on the class.

uses.description: Without the support argument, returns the number of support abilities listed on the named actor class. With both arguments, returns 1 if the named class teaches the given support ability and 0 otherwise.

arguments.name: classId
arguments.description: Identifier of an actor class whose support abilities list is inspected.

arguments.name: supportAbilityId
arguments.description: Optional support ability identifier. When provided, returns 1 if the class teaches this ability; when omitted, the operator returns the total number of support abilities on the class.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the player has discovered/unlocked the named actor class (and the class is not a 'dummy' placeholder), otherwise 0. Used to gate logic on whether a class is available to the player.

arguments.name: classId
arguments.description: Identifier of an actor class to check against the game state's known-classes list.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the caster meets all of the requirements defined on the named actor class (race, prerequisites, etc.) and could therefore use it, otherwise 0.

arguments.name: classId
arguments.description: Identifier of an actor class whose usability is tested against the caster.

uses.description: Counts how many of the named actor class's actions the caster has purchased at least one rank in. Useful for displaying or gating on the caster's progress in a class.

arguments.name: classId
arguments.description: Identifier of an actor class whose actions list is checked against the caster's purchased ranks.

uses.description: Counts how many of the named actor class's support abilities the caster has purchased at least one rank in. Useful for displaying or gating on the caster's progress in a class.

arguments.name: classId
arguments.description: Identifier of an actor class whose support abilities list is checked against the caster's purchased ranks.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the caster has purchased the maximum rank in every action belonging to the named actor class, otherwise 0. A 'mastery' check for class actions.

arguments.name: classId
arguments.description: Identifier of an actor class whose actions must all be fully ranked on the caster.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the caster has purchased the maximum rank in every support ability belonging to the named actor class, otherwise 0. A 'mastery' check for class support abilities.

arguments.name: classId
arguments.description: Identifier of an actor class whose support abilities must all be fully ranked on the caster.

uses.description: Returns 1 if an actor of the specified ID exists in the current zone and is currently a party member, otherwise 0. The lookup excludes fleet-only actors.

arguments.name: actorId
arguments.description: Identifier of an actor to check against the current party roster.

uses.description: Returns 1 if any party actor is standing on the specified tile coordinate, otherwise 0. Used to detect that a party member occupies a particular location.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: X coordinate to check against party actor positions. Parsed as an integer.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: Y coordinate to check against party actor positions. Parsed as an integer.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the actor ID is included in the current landing party list (the subset of crew designated to disembark), otherwise 0.

arguments.name: actorId
arguments.description: Identifier of an actor to check against the landing party roster.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named actor is a current party member or a member of the player fleet's crew, otherwise 0. Useful for checks that should accept any actor controlled by the player.

arguments.name: actorId
arguments.description: Identifier of an actor to check against the party roster and the player fleet crew.

uses.description: Returns 1 if any boat in the player's fleet is of the specified actor type, otherwise 0. Useful for checking whether the player owns at least one ship of a particular class.

arguments.name: actorTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of the ship actor type to look for in the player fleet.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the party member at the specified index is the main player character, otherwise 0. Used to determine whether the controllable lead character occupies a particular party slot.

arguments.name: index
arguments.description: Zero-based index into the party actors list. Parsed as an integer.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the main player character's actor type matches the specified value, otherwise 0. Used to gate logic on the lead character's species/class.

arguments.name: actorTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of an actor type to compare against the main player's actorTypeID.

uses.description: Sums the named actor value across every party actor. Useful for computing aggregate party stats such as combined HP or combined skill totals.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the actor value to read from each party actor and sum together.

uses.description: Sums the named actor value across every crew member of the player's fleet who is NOT also in the current party. Useful for aggregating stats over the non-party crew (e.g. total morale or skill).

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of the actor value to read from each non-party crew member and sum together.

uses.description: Returns the number of times the caster has used the named action over their tracked lifetime. Useful for actions that scale or trigger based on repeated use.

arguments.name: actionId
arguments.description: Identifier of the action whose use count by the caster is queried.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the actor currently grabbed by the cursor (the held actor) has a property matching the specified value, otherwise 0. Useful for input-driven checks on what the player is dragging.

arguments.name: property
arguments.description: Name of a property/field on the Actor to check.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Value to compare the property against; supports equality, list-contains, and enum flag checks via DataManager.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the actor currently grabbed by the cursor (the held actor) has an actor type whose property matches the specified value, otherwise 0. Useful for checking actor-type fields on the dragged actor.

arguments.name: property
arguments.description: Name of a property/field on the held actor's actorType to check.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Value to compare the property against; supports equality, list-contains, and enum flag checks via DataManager.

uses.description: Evaluates a named global formula (defined in the formulaGlobals data table) and returns its result. Used to delegate to a reusable named formula rather than inlining its body.

arguments.name: formulaId
arguments.description: Identifier of an entry in the formulaGlobals data table whose formula is evaluated with the current caster, target, rank, x, and targetTC.

uses.description: Evaluates a named global formula and clamps the result to be at least 0 (so any negative result becomes 0). A convenience for the common pattern of disallowing negative outputs.

arguments.name: formulaId
arguments.description: Identifier of an entry in the formulaGlobals data table whose formula is evaluated with the current caster, target, rank, x, and targetTC; the result is floored at 0.

uses.description: Evaluates a named global formula and clamps the result to be at most 0 (so any positive result becomes 0). Used when the output must be non-positive.

arguments.name: formulaId
arguments.description: Identifier of an entry in the formulaGlobals data table whose formula is evaluated with the current caster, target, rank, x, and targetTC; the result is capped at 0.

uses.description: Evaluates a named global formula and clamps the result to be at most 1. Used when the output must not exceed 1 (e.g., probability-like values).

arguments.name: formulaId
arguments.description: Identifier of an entry in the formulaGlobals data table whose formula is evaluated with the current caster, target, rank, x, and targetTC; the result is capped at 1.

uses.description: Evaluates a named global formula and clamps the result to be at most -1. Used when the output must be -1 or smaller.

arguments.name: formulaId
arguments.description: Identifier of an entry in the formulaGlobals data table whose formula is evaluated with the current caster, target, rank, x, and targetTC; the result is capped at -1.

uses.description: Evaluates a named global formula and clamps the result to be at most the specified ceiling. Despite the name, this acts as a maximum cap.

arguments.name: formulaId
arguments.description: Identifier of an entry in the formulaGlobals data table whose formula is evaluated with the current caster, target, rank, x, and targetTC.

arguments.name: ceiling
arguments.description: Numeric ceiling applied via Math.Min to the evaluated formula result.

uses.description: Evaluates a named global formula and clamps the result to be at least the specified floor. Despite the name, this acts as a minimum floor.

arguments.name: formulaId
arguments.description: Identifier of an entry in the formulaGlobals data table whose formula is evaluated with the current caster, target, rank, x, and targetTC.

arguments.name: floor
arguments.description: Numeric floor applied via Math.Max to the evaluated formula result.

uses.description: If the caster has the named support ability, evaluates the second-named global formula and returns its result; otherwise returns 0. Useful for conditionally applying bonus formulas based on owned support abilities.

arguments.name: supportAbilityId
arguments.description: Identifier of a support ability the caster must possess for the bonus formula to be evaluated.

arguments.name: formulaId
arguments.description: Identifier of an entry in the formulaGlobals data table whose formula is evaluated only when the support ability check passes.

uses.description: Dispatches to a math sub-operator (one of the operators defined in evaluateMath). The argument names the specific math operator to invoke; all 'm:' operators are documented separately with the 'm:' prefix.

arguments.name: mathOperator
arguments.description: Name of a math sub-operator to dispatch to. See the m: prefixed entries for the full list of available math operators.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named math sub-operator evaluates to 0, otherwise 0. Wraps a math dispatch with an equality-zero check.

arguments.name: mathOperator
arguments.description: Name of a math sub-operator (from evaluateMath) whose result is tested for being exactly zero.

uses.description: Dispatches to a math sub-operator and clamps its result to be at least 0 (so any negative result becomes 0). A convenience wrapper for math operators that need to be non-negative.

arguments.name: mathOperator
arguments.description: Name of a math sub-operator (from evaluateMath) whose result is floored at 0.

uses.description: Returns the product of all damage multipliers in the target's reactions to the specified element. Multiplicatively combines every weakness, resistance, and immunity entry that triggers on that element. A value below 1 indicates resistance, above 1 indicates weakness, and 0 indicates immunity.

arguments.name: element
arguments.description: Name of an Element enum value (such as fire, ice, melee, magic).

uses.description: Sums the floored damage-mod formulas attached to every reaction the target has against the specified element. Used to compute the additive damage modifier from elemental reactions (e.g., flat bonus damage from a weakness).

arguments.name: element
arguments.description: Name of an Element enum value to query for matching reactions on the target.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the target's combined damage multiplier against the specified element is 0 or negative (i.e., they are fully immune or healed by it), otherwise 0.

arguments.name: element
arguments.description: Name of an Element enum value whose multiplicative damage effect on the target is tested for immunity.

uses.description: Returns the product of all damage multipliers in the caster's reactions to the specified element. The caster-side equivalent of ERxn: a value below 1 indicates the caster resists the element, above 1 indicates weakness, and 0 indicates immunity.

arguments.name: element
arguments.description: Name of an Element enum value (such as fire, ice, melee, magic).

uses.description: Sums the floored damage-mod formulas attached to every reaction the caster has against the specified element. Caster-side equivalent of rxnMod.

arguments.name: element
arguments.description: Name of an Element enum value to query for matching reactions on the caster.

uses.description: Returns the product of all damage multipliers in the target's reactions matching the specified actor value (used for actor-value-keyed reactions rather than element-keyed). Searches reactions across all elements that mention the given actor value.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of an actor value to match against the target's actor-value-keyed reactions.

uses.description: Sums the floored damage-mod formulas attached to every actor-value-keyed reaction the target has matching the specified actor value. The actor-value-keyed equivalent of rxnMod.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of an actor value to match against the target's actor-value-keyed reactions.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the target's combined damage multiplier across actor-value-keyed reactions matching the specified actor value is 0 or negative, otherwise 0. Equivalent of rxnImmune for actor-value-keyed reactions.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of an actor value whose multiplicative reaction effect on the target is tested for immunity.

uses.description: Returns the product of all damage multipliers in the caster's reactions matching the specified actor value. Caster-side equivalent of AVrxn.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of an actor value to match against the caster's actor-value-keyed reactions.

uses.description: Sums the floored damage-mod formulas attached to every actor-value-keyed reaction the caster has matching the specified actor value. Caster-side equivalent of AVrxnMod.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of an actor value to match against the caster's actor-value-keyed reactions.

uses.description: Counts how many elemental reactions on the target match the specified element. Useful for checking the breadth of a target's interactions with a particular element.

arguments.name: element
arguments.description: Name of an Element enum value to match against the target's reactions.

uses.description: Counts how many elemental reactions on the target tile (across all items and terrain present) match the specified element. Useful for measuring how reactive a tile is to a particular element.

arguments.name: element
arguments.description: Name of an Element enum value to match against the target tile's reactions.

uses.description: Without the filter argument, returns the total number of reactions defined on the named item type. With the filter argument, counts reactions whose element list contains the named element OR whose actorValues list contains the given actor value string.

arguments.name: itemTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of an item type in the data tables whose reactions list is inspected.

arguments.name: filter
arguments.description: Optional filter; either an Element enum name (matched against each reaction's element list) or any string (matched against actor value entries in the reaction).

uses.description: Without the filter argument, returns the total number of in-weapon reactions defined on the named item type. With the filter argument, counts in-weapon reactions whose element list contains the named element OR whose actorValues list contains the given actor value string.

arguments.name: itemTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of an item type whose reactions_inWeapon list is inspected.

arguments.name: filter
arguments.description: Optional filter; either an Element enum name (matched against each reaction's element list) or any string (matched against actor value entries in the reaction).

uses.description: Without the filter argument, returns the total number of in-armor reactions defined on the named item type. With the filter argument, counts in-armor reactions whose element list contains the named element OR whose actorValues list contains the given actor value string.

arguments.name: itemTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of an item type whose reactions_inArmor list is inspected.

arguments.name: filter
arguments.description: Optional filter; either an Element enum name (matched against each reaction's element list) or any string (matched against actor value entries in the reaction).

uses.description: Without the filter argument, returns the total number of reactions defined on the named actor type. With the filter argument, counts reactions whose element list contains the named element OR whose actorValues list contains the given actor value string.

arguments.name: actorTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of an actor type in the data tables whose reactions list is inspected.

arguments.name: filter
arguments.description: Optional filter; either an Element enum name (matched against each reaction's element list) or any string (matched against actor value entries in the reaction).

uses.description: Without the filter argument, returns the total number of reactions defined on the named actor class. With the filter argument, counts reactions whose element list contains the named element OR whose actorValues list contains the given actor value string.

arguments.name: classId
arguments.description: Identifier of an actor class whose reactions list is inspected.

arguments.name: filter
arguments.description: Optional filter; either an Element enum name (matched against each reaction's element list) or any string (matched against actor value entries in the reaction).

uses.description: Without the filter argument, returns the total number of reactions defined on the named support ability. With the filter argument, counts reactions whose element list contains the named element OR whose actorValues list contains the given actor value string.

arguments.name: supportAbilityId
arguments.description: Identifier of a support ability whose reactions list is inspected.

arguments.name: filter
arguments.description: Optional filter; either an Element enum name (matched against each reaction's element list) or any string (matched against actor value entries in the reaction).

uses.description: Reads a global variable whose name is constructed by concatenating a fixed prefix with the string value of another global variable's name. Useful for indexed global lookups where the key is itself stored in a variable.

arguments.name: prefix
arguments.description: Fixed prefix appended before the dereferenced global variable's value to form the target global key.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a string global variable; its current value is concatenated after the prefix to form the actual key looked up.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the indirectly-keyed global variable (built by concatenating a prefix with another global's string value) equals exactly 1, otherwise 0. Boolean form of g_gID.

arguments.name: prefix
arguments.description: Fixed prefix appended before the dereferenced global variable's value to form the target global key.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a string global variable; its current value is concatenated after the prefix to form the actual key looked up.

uses.description: Reads a global variable whose name is constructed by concatenating a fixed prefix with the current location's ID. Used to scope a global value to the currently-visited location.

arguments.name: prefix
arguments.description: Fixed prefix appended before the current location's ID to form the target global key.

uses.description: Returns the numeric value of a named global variable. Returns 0 if the global variable does not exist.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a global variable whose numeric value is returned.

uses.description: Returns the numeric value of a named global variable clamped to be at most 1 (so any value 1 or above returns 1, and fractional values pass through). Returns 0 if the global variable does not exist. Used for boolean-like reads of counter globals.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a global variable whose numeric value (capped at 1) is returned.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named global variable equals exactly 0 (or does not exist, since getGlobalVar returns 0 for missing keys), otherwise 0.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a global variable whose value is tested for equality with 0.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named global variable exists and equals exactly 1, otherwise 0. Used as a boolean check on flag-style globals.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a global variable whose value is tested for equality with 1.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named global variable exists and equals exactly 2, otherwise 0. Useful for multi-state flags where 2 represents a specific stage.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a global variable whose value is tested for equality with 2.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named global variable's string value matches the ID of the actor the player is currently in dialog with, otherwise 0. Useful for branching on whether a previously-saved actor matches the current conversation partner.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a global variable whose string value is compared against the current conversation partner's ID.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named global variable's string value matches the ID of the player's current location, otherwise 0. Useful for branching on whether a saved location matches where the player is now.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a global variable whose string value is compared against the current location's ID.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named global variable's string value matches the faction ID of the player's current location, otherwise 0. Used for checking that a stored faction matches the local political ownership.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a global variable whose string value is compared against the current location's faction ID.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named global variable's string value matches the faction of the actor the player is currently in dialog with, otherwise 0. Useful for branching on whether the current conversation partner's faction matches a stored value.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a global variable whose string value is compared against the current conversation partner's faction.

uses.description: Returns 1 if any actor in the current zone has the specified dialog node ID and that actor's ID matches the value of the named global variable, otherwise 0. Used to test that a stored actor ID belongs to an actor currently waiting on a particular dialog node.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a string global variable whose value is treated as an actor ID.

arguments.name: dialogNodeId
arguments.description: Dialog node identifier to match against actors in the current zone.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the location referenced by the named global variable (whose string value is used as a location ID) has been discovered by the player, otherwise 0. Used for goal/quest conditions tied to a dynamically-stored location.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a string global variable whose value is treated as a location identifier.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the location referenced by the named global variable (whose string value is used as a location ID) has the specified tag, otherwise 0. Used to test categorical tags on a dynamically-stored location.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a string global variable whose value is treated as a location identifier.

arguments.name: tag
arguments.description: Tag string to look for in the resolved location's tags list.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named global variable's string value equals the specified comparison value, otherwise 0. Supports a comma-packed shorthand where the first argument is 'globalVarName,value' on a single segment.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a global variable to compare. Alternatively, a comma-separated 'name,value' pair packed into this single segment (omitting the value argument).

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Comparison value. Required when the first argument is plain (not the comma-packed form).

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named global variable's string value does not equal the specified comparison value, otherwise 0. Supports the same comma-packed shorthand as gIs.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a global variable to compare. Alternatively, a comma-separated 'name,value' pair packed into this single segment (omitting the value argument).

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Comparison value. Required when the first argument is plain (not the comma-packed form).

uses.description: Returns 1 if the numeric value of the named global variable is strictly less than the specified threshold, otherwise 0.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a global variable whose numeric value is tested.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Numeric threshold to compare the global against; returns 1 only when the global is strictly less.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the numeric value of the named global variable is strictly greater than the specified threshold, otherwise 0.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a global variable whose numeric value is tested.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Numeric threshold to compare the global against; returns 1 only when the global is strictly greater.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named global variable holds a non-empty string value that cannot be parsed as a number and is not '0', otherwise 0. Used to distinguish 'has a meaningful string assigned' from numeric or unset globals.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a global variable to test for being a non-numeric, non-empty, non-'0' string.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named global variable's string value matches the ID of the party member at the specified index, otherwise 0. Useful for checking whether a stored actor reference matches a particular party slot.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a string global variable whose value is treated as an actor ID.

arguments.name: index
arguments.description: Zero-based party index. Parsed as an integer.

uses.description: Returns the world-time timestamp recorded when the named global variable was last assigned. Useful for time-since-event calculations relative to absolute world time.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a global variable whose assignment timestamp is read.

uses.description: Returns the elapsed world time since the named global variable was last assigned. Useful for measuring how long ago an event occurred.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a global variable whose elapsed-time-since-assignment is returned.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the elapsed world time since the named global variable was last assigned is strictly less than the specified threshold, otherwise 0. Returns 0 if the global variable does not exist.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a global variable whose elapsed-time-since-assignment is tested.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Elapsed-time threshold to compare against.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the elapsed world time since the named global variable was last assigned is less than or equal to the specified threshold, otherwise 0. Returns 0 if the global variable does not exist.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a global variable whose elapsed-time-since-assignment is tested.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Elapsed-time threshold to compare against.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named global variable has never been assigned, or if the elapsed world time since its last assignment is strictly greater than the threshold, otherwise 0. Useful for cooldown checks where 'never used' should pass.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a global variable whose elapsed-time-since-assignment is tested (or returns 1 if the global is unset).

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Elapsed-time threshold to compare against.
