uses.description: Returns 1 if the elapsed world time since the named global variable was last assigned is strictly greater than the threshold, otherwise 0. Returns 0 if the global variable does not exist.

arguments.name: globalVar
arguments.description: Name of a global variable whose elapsed-time-since-assignment is tested.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Elapsed-time threshold to compare against.

uses.description: Returns the elapsed world time since a global variable (whose key is built by concatenating a prefix with the current location's ID) was last assigned. Used for measuring location-scoped event recency.

arguments.name: prefix
arguments.description: Fixed prefix appended before the current location's ID to form the target global key.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the string values of the two named global variables are identical, otherwise 0. Used to compare one global against another (rather than against a literal).

arguments.name: globalVarA
arguments.description: Name of the first global variable to compare.

arguments.name: globalVarB
arguments.description: Name of the second global variable to compare.

uses.description: Returns the strength of the named element in the area around the caster's tile, sampled within a radius of 2 tiles (capped at 3 maximum strength) and showing the sampling visual effect. Both the radius and the maximum strength can be overridden via the optional arguments.

arguments.name: element
arguments.description: Name of an Element enum value whose area strength is sampled.

arguments.name: radius
arguments.description: Optional sampling radius in tiles. Defaults to 2 if omitted.

arguments.name: maxStrength
arguments.description: Optional cap on the returned strength value. Defaults to 3 if omitted.

uses.description: Returns the strength of the named element in the area around the caster's tile, sampled within a radius of 2 tiles (capped at 3 maximum strength) and showing the sampling visual effect. Behaves identically to geo unless the optional radius/maxStrength arguments override the defaults.

arguments.name: element
arguments.description: Name of an Element enum value whose area strength is sampled.

arguments.name: radius
arguments.description: Optional sampling radius in tiles. Defaults to 2 if omitted.

arguments.name: maxStrength
arguments.description: Optional cap on the returned strength value. Defaults to 3 if omitted.

uses.description: Same as geo (samples element strength in a radius of 2 around the caster, capped at 3) but suppresses the visual sampling effect. Used for silent/background sampling.

arguments.name: element
arguments.description: Name of an Element enum value whose area strength is sampled.

arguments.name: radius
arguments.description: Optional sampling radius in tiles. Defaults to 2 if omitted.

arguments.name: maxStrength
arguments.description: Optional cap on the returned strength value. Defaults to 3 if omitted.

uses.description: Same as geo2 (samples element strength in a radius of 2 around the caster, capped at 3) but suppresses the visual sampling effect. Used for silent/background sampling.

arguments.name: element
arguments.description: Name of an Element enum value whose area strength is sampled.

arguments.name: radius
arguments.description: Optional sampling radius in tiles. Defaults to 2 if omitted.

arguments.name: maxStrength
arguments.description: Optional cap on the returned strength value. Defaults to 3 if omitted.

uses.description: Returns the strength of the named element in the area around the caster's tile, sampled within a radius of 1 tile (capped at 3 maximum strength). The radius default differs from geo; the radius and max strength can still be overridden.

arguments.name: element
arguments.description: Name of an Element enum value whose area strength is sampled.

arguments.name: radius
arguments.description: Optional sampling radius in tiles. Defaults to 1 for this operator if omitted.

arguments.name: maxStrength
arguments.description: Optional cap on the returned strength value. Defaults to 3 if omitted.

uses.description: Returns the strength of the named element exactly at the caster's tile (radius 0), capped at 3. Useful for checking whether the caster is standing in a specific element without any neighbor blending.

arguments.name: element
arguments.description: Name of an Element enum value whose strength on the caster's tile is sampled.

arguments.name: radius
arguments.description: Optional sampling radius in tiles. Defaults to 0 for this operator if omitted.

arguments.name: maxStrength
arguments.description: Optional cap on the returned strength value. Defaults to 3 if omitted.

uses.description: Returns the strength of the named element in the area around the target's tile (or targetTC if no target actor is set), sampled within a radius of 2 tiles (capped at 3 maximum strength) and showing the sampling visual effect.

arguments.name: element
arguments.description: Name of an Element enum value whose area strength is sampled around the target.

arguments.name: radius
arguments.description: Optional sampling radius in tiles. Defaults to 2 if omitted.

arguments.name: maxStrength
arguments.description: Optional cap on the returned strength value. Defaults to 3 if omitted.

uses.description: Same as geoTarget (samples element strength in a radius of 2 around the target, capped at 3) but suppresses the visual sampling effect. Used for silent/background sampling.

arguments.name: element
arguments.description: Name of an Element enum value whose area strength is sampled around the target.

arguments.name: radius
arguments.description: Optional sampling radius in tiles. Defaults to 2 if omitted.

arguments.name: maxStrength
arguments.description: Optional cap on the returned strength value. Defaults to 3 if omitted.

uses.description: Returns the strength of the named element at the specified tile coordinate, sampled with radius 0 and capped at maximum strength 3, with no visual effects shown. Used for precise location-based element checks.

arguments.name: element
arguments.description: Name of an Element enum value whose strength on the given tile is queried.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: X coordinate of the tile to query. Parsed as an integer.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: Y coordinate of the tile to query. Parsed as an integer.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the caster's favored-hand weapon contains an augment item (in its container) whose ItemType has the specified element, otherwise 0. Used to detect weapon enchantments by element.

arguments.name: element
arguments.description: Name of an Element enum value to match against the augmenting item's element.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the caster's favored-hand weapon has an augment item with the specified element AND the caster does not have a corresponding '<element>Wpn' actor value buff active, otherwise 0. Useful for distinguishing innate augments from temporary enchantments.

arguments.name: element
arguments.description: Name of an Element enum value to match against the augment, while also ruling out an enchanted weapon buff of the same element.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named zone has fog-of-war data recorded (i.e., the player has visited it at least once), otherwise 0. Used to detect whether a zone has been entered before.

arguments.name: zoneName
arguments.description: Name of a zone whose fog-of-war presence is checked.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the player is currently in the named zone, otherwise 0. Useful for gating logic on the player's current location at the zone level.

arguments.name: zoneName
arguments.description: Name of a zone to compare against the player's current zone name.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the terrain type on the target tile (or targetTC if no target actor is set) has the specified property matching the given value, otherwise 0. Used to test fields, list-membership, or enum flags on the terrain type.

arguments.name: property
arguments.description: Name of a property/field on the TerrainType to check.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Value to compare the property against; supports equality, list-contains, and enum flag checks via DataManager.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the terrain type on the target tile (or targetTC if no target actor is set) has the specified element as its terrainElement, otherwise 0.

arguments.name: element
arguments.description: Name of an Element enum value to compare against the terrain's terrainElement.

uses.description: Counts the reactions on the target tile's terrain type (or targetTC if no target actor is set) whose element list contains the specified element OR whose actorValues list contains the given string. Useful for measuring how reactive a terrain is to an element or actor value.

arguments.name: filter
arguments.description: Either an Element enum name (matched against each reaction's element list) or any string (matched against actor value entries in the reaction).

uses.description: Returns the percentage of the named zone that the player has explored, as a number from 0 to 100 (rounded to two decimals). Computed from the fog-of-war texture.

arguments.name: zoneName
arguments.description: Name of a zone whose explored percentage is computed.

uses.description: Counts the active triggers in the current zone whose area contains the target's tile (or targetTC) and whose triggeredByElement list includes the specified element (or any element if 'any' is given). Active means the trigger's preconditions currently pass.

arguments.name: element
arguments.description: Name of an Element enum value to filter triggers by; use 'any' to match all elements.

uses.description: Counts the currently-disabled triggers in the current zone whose area contains the target's tile (or targetTC) and whose triggeredByElement list includes the specified element (or any element if 'any' is given). Disabled means the trigger's preconditions fail right now.

arguments.name: element
arguments.description: Name of an Element enum value to filter triggers by; use 'any' to match all elements.

uses.description: Counts the active triggers anywhere in the current zone whose triggeredByElement list includes the specified element (or any element if 'any' is given). Like activeTriggersTarget but does not restrict by area.

arguments.name: element
arguments.description: Name of an Element enum value to filter triggers by; use 'any' to match all elements.

uses.description: Counts the currently-disabled triggers anywhere in the current zone whose triggeredByElement list includes the specified element (or any element if 'any' is given). Like disabledTriggersTarget but does not restrict by area.

arguments.name: element
arguments.description: Name of an Element enum value to filter triggers by; use 'any' to match all elements.

uses.description: Counts the trigger effects on the specified trigger. Without the effectID argument, counts all trigger effects; with the effectID argument, only counts effects whose effectID matches. The trigger is looked up first in the current zone's trigger list (by numID) and then in the global triggers data table.

arguments.name: triggerNumId
arguments.description: Numeric identifier of a trigger (or a key into the globalTriggers data table) whose effects list is inspected.

arguments.name: effectId
arguments.description: Optional effect identifier; when provided, only effects whose effectID matches this string are counted.

uses.description: Returns the maximum delay across all trigger effects on the specified trigger. The trigger is looked up first in the current zone's trigger list (by numID) and then in the global triggers data table. Useful for estimating how long after triggering all effects will have fired.

arguments.name: triggerNumId
arguments.description: Numeric identifier of a trigger (or a key into the globalTriggers data table) whose effects' delays are scanned.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the specified trigger has the named property matching the given value, otherwise 0. The trigger is looked up first in the current zone's trigger list (by numID) and then in the global triggers data table.

arguments.name: triggerNumId
arguments.description: Numeric identifier of a trigger (or a key into the globalTriggers data table).

arguments.name: property
arguments.description: Name of a property/field on the Trigger to check.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Value to compare the property against; supports equality, list-contains, and enum flag checks via DataManager.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the player's current location ID matches the specified value, otherwise 0. Used to gate logic on the named location the player is currently visiting.

arguments.name: locationId
arguments.description: Identifier of a location to compare against the player's current location.

uses.description: With two arguments, returns the numeric value of the named field on the specified location. With one argument, returns that field's numeric value on the player's current location. Useful for reading numeric properties off Location data.

arguments.name: locationIdOrField
arguments.description: Either a location identifier (when used with the field argument) or the field name (when used in the one-argument form to read from the current location).

arguments.name: field
arguments.description: Optional field name. Required when the two-argument form is used; specifies the numeric field to read from the named location.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named location is known/discovered by the player, otherwise 0. Used to gate logic on whether a specific location has been found.

arguments.name: locationId
arguments.description: Identifier of a location whose discovered flag is checked.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named location is marked discoverable and its discoverable-requirements formula passes when evaluated against the main party member, otherwise 0. Used to test whether the player currently meets the conditions to find a location.

arguments.name: locationId
arguments.description: Identifier of a location whose discoverability conditions are tested.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named location's wealth level is 100 or higher, otherwise 0. Used as a shorthand check that a port has reached the wealthiest tier.

arguments.name: locationId
arguments.description: Identifier of a location whose wealthLevel is compared against 100.

uses.description: Returns the total quantity of an item at the specified location, searching its shipwright list, stash, and cargo. Returns 1 (or 9999 for unlimited-quantity entries) if the item exists in shipwright_boatTypes. Returns 0 if the location does not exist or has none of the item.

arguments.name: locationId
arguments.description: Identifier of a location whose inventories are searched.

arguments.name: itemTypeId
arguments.description: Identifier of the item type to count at the location.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named location's auto-spawn data list contains the specified spawn data ID, otherwise 0. Used to check that a location is configured to auto-spawn a particular content set.

arguments.name: locationId
arguments.description: Identifier of a location whose auto_spawnDataID list is inspected.

arguments.name: spawnDataId
arguments.description: Spawn data identifier to look for in the location's auto_spawnDataID list.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named location offers a quest of the specified quest type (and optionally with the specified quest goal), otherwise 0. Used to check that a location is offering a particular kind of work to the player.

arguments.name: locationId
arguments.description: Identifier of a location whose questTypes/questGoals lists are inspected.

arguments.name: questType
arguments.description: Quest type identifier that the location must offer.

arguments.name: questGoal
arguments.description: Optional quest goal identifier; when provided, only matches if a quest of the given type also has this goal.

uses.description: Returns the X overworld coordinate of the named location. Returns 0 if the location does not exist.

arguments.name: locationId
arguments.description: Identifier of a location whose X coordinate is returned.

uses.description: Returns the Y overworld coordinate of the named location. Returns 0 if the location does not exist.

arguments.name: locationId
arguments.description: Identifier of a location whose Y coordinate is returned.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named location's tooltip icons list contains the specified icon identifier, otherwise 0. Used to check whether a location is displaying a particular status icon.

arguments.name: locationId
arguments.description: Identifier of a location whose tooltipIcons list is inspected.

arguments.name: iconId
arguments.description: Icon identifier to look for in the location's tooltip icons.

uses.description: Returns the number of items currently in the named location's stash inventory. Useful for checking how much loot a location is holding.

arguments.name: locationId
arguments.description: Identifier of a location whose stash inventory item count is returned.

uses.description: Returns the number of items currently in the named location's cargo inventory. Useful for checking how full a port's trading cargo is.

arguments.name: locationId
arguments.description: Identifier of a location whose cargo inventory item count is returned.

uses.description: Returns the number of ship types currently offered by the named location's shipwright. Returns 0 if the location does not exist.

arguments.name: locationId
arguments.description: Identifier of a location whose shipwright_boatTypes count is returned.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named location's stash inventory has reached its maximum slot capacity, otherwise 0. Used to check whether a stash can accept any more items.

arguments.name: locationId
arguments.description: Identifier of a location whose stash fullness is checked.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named location's cargo inventory has reached its maximum slot capacity, otherwise 0. Used to check whether a port's cargo can accept any more items.

arguments.name: locationId
arguments.description: Identifier of a location whose cargo fullness is checked.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named location's shipwright already lists 8 or more ship types (its hardcoded cap), otherwise 0. Useful for gating offerings of new ship types at a port.

arguments.name: locationId
arguments.description: Identifier of a location whose shipwright count is compared against the cap of 8.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named location has the specified property matching the given value, otherwise 0. Used to test fields, list-membership, or enum flags on a Location data entry.

arguments.name: locationId
arguments.description: Identifier of a location whose properties are tested.

arguments.name: property
arguments.description: Name of a property/field on the Location to check.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Value to compare the property against; supports equality, list-contains, and enum flag checks via DataManager.

uses.description: Returns the player's favor with the faction that owns the named location, clamped at a minimum of -99. Read from the global variable 'favor_<faction>'.

arguments.name: locationId
arguments.description: Identifier of a location whose owning faction's favor with the player is returned.

uses.description: Returns the named faction's favor with the player at the specified location. Returns -9999 if the result would be 0 and the location is marked as everloyal (treating the faction as deeply unfavorable in that context).

arguments.name: locationId
arguments.description: Identifier of a location whose faction-favor lookup is performed.

arguments.name: factionId
arguments.description: Identifier of the faction whose favor at the location is returned.

uses.description: Returns the per-day restock count of trade goods at the named location, computed from its wealth level and the value of its trade good's item type. The value is clamped to at least 1 and at least 2 if the location is at maximum wealth.

arguments.name: locationId
arguments.description: Identifier of a location whose trade good restock rate is returned.

uses.description: Returns the per-unit buy price of the named location's cafe food item. Useful for tooltip pricing previews.

arguments.name: locationId
arguments.description: Identifier of a location whose cafe food per-unit price is returned.

uses.description: Returns the total cost of buying enough cafe food at the named location to feed the player's full fleet crew. Equivalent to the per-unit food price multiplied by the player fleet's crew count.

arguments.name: locationId
arguments.description: Identifier of a location whose total food-feeding cost is returned.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the player has enough money to feed the entire fleet crew at the named location's cafe, otherwise 0.

arguments.name: locationId
arguments.description: Identifier of a location whose total food-feeding cost is compared against the player's money.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named location's itemType_formula_unlockable mapping contains any key that matches an actor type ID (i.e., a ship is unlockable at that location), otherwise 0.

arguments.name: locationId
arguments.description: Identifier of a location whose unlockable items are scanned for ship-actor-types.

uses.description: Returns the maximum favor value the player can currently reach with the named faction. Useful for displaying the favor cap.

arguments.name: factionId
arguments.description: Identifier of a faction whose maximum-achievable favor is returned.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the player's combined fame (the sum of explore, trade, and combat fame globals) is strictly less than the specified threshold, otherwise 0.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Numeric threshold to compare the player's combined fame against.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the player's combined fame (the sum of explore, trade, and combat fame globals) is strictly more than the specified threshold, otherwise 0.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Numeric threshold to compare the player's combined fame against.

uses.description: Returns the number of allied ports controlled by the named faction. Useful for tracking faction expansion.

arguments.name: factionId
arguments.description: Identifier of a faction whose allied-port count is returned.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named faction's number of allied ports is strictly greater than the specified threshold, otherwise 0.

arguments.name: factionId
arguments.description: Identifier of a faction whose allied-port count is queried.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Integer threshold for the port count comparison.

uses.description: Returns the aggregated wealth value of the named faction. Useful for displaying or scaling rewards by faction prosperity.

arguments.name: factionId
arguments.description: Identifier of a faction whose wealth is returned.

uses.description: Returns the total number of fleets (merchant + battle) currently associated with the named faction.

arguments.name: factionId
arguments.description: Identifier of a faction whose fleet count is returned.

uses.description: Returns the number of merchant-type fleets currently associated with the named faction (battle fleets are excluded).

arguments.name: factionId
arguments.description: Identifier of a faction whose merchant fleet count is returned.

uses.description: Returns the number of battle-type fleets currently associated with the named faction (merchant fleets are excluded).

arguments.name: factionId
arguments.description: Identifier of a faction whose battle fleet count is returned.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named faction has the specified property matching the given value, otherwise 0. Used to test fields, list-membership, or enum flags on a Faction data entry.

arguments.name: factionId
arguments.description: Identifier of a faction in the data tables.

arguments.name: property
arguments.description: Name of a property/field on the Faction to check.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Value to compare the property against; supports equality, list-contains, and enum flag checks via DataManager.

uses.description: Returns the numeric value of a named field on the specified faction, parsed as a float. Useful for reading numeric properties on faction data entries.

arguments.name: factionId
arguments.description: Identifier of a faction in the data tables.

arguments.name: property
arguments.description: Name of a numeric field on the Faction to look up and parse as a float.

uses.description: Returns the experience earned by the party member at the specified index in their currently equipped class. Useful for displaying or gating logic on a party member's progress.

arguments.name: index
arguments.description: Zero-based party index. Parsed as an integer.

uses.description: Returns the total XP earned by the actor in the current zone whose dialogNodeID matches the specified value (typically a vendor). Useful for vendor-progression checks.

arguments.name: dialogNodeId
arguments.description: Dialog node identifier identifying the vendor actor whose total earned XP is returned.

uses.description: Returns 1 if any actor in the current zone has the specified dialog node ID, otherwise 0. Useful for detecting the presence of a specific NPC role (e.g., 'is there a blacksmith here?').

arguments.name: dialogNodeId
arguments.description: Dialog node identifier to look for among actors in the current zone.

uses.description: Counts how many actors in the current zone have the specified dialog node ID. Useful for measuring how many NPCs in a role are present.

arguments.name: dialogNodeId
arguments.description: Dialog node identifier to look for among actors in the current zone.

uses.description: Returns 1 if a deterministic 'random' number derived from the target's hash code and the current world day (in 6-day chunks) — uniformly drawn from [0, max] inclusive — equals the specified expected value, otherwise 0. Also has a side effect of seeding Game1.random to that deterministic seed and setting x to max.

arguments.name: max
arguments.description: Maximum value of the rolled integer (inclusive). Parsed as an integer; clamped to at least 1.

arguments.name: expected
arguments.description: Value the rolled integer must equal for this operator to return 1. Parsed as an integer.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named journal entry has been added to the player's known journal, otherwise 0. Also matches when the argument resolves to a known item-type, actor-type, or actor-class whose journal ID has been collected.

arguments.name: journalIdOrTypeId
arguments.description: Journal entry identifier or the ID of an item type, actor type, or actor class whose associated journal entry is checked.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named journal entry has been sold/reported to a vendor, otherwise 0. Also matches when the argument resolves to a known item-type, actor-type, or actor-class whose journal ID has been reported.

arguments.name: journalIdOrTypeId
arguments.description: Journal entry identifier or the ID of an item type, actor type, or actor class whose associated journal entry is checked.

uses.description: Returns the rarity tier of the named journal entry (or the journal entry of a matching item type, actor type, or actor class). Returns -1 if the entry cannot be found.

arguments.name: journalIdOrTypeId
arguments.description: Journal entry identifier or the ID of an item type, actor type, or actor class whose associated journal rarity is returned.

uses.description: Returns the reward value of the named journal entry (computed from the rarity-tier reward plus the entry's reward modifier). Returns 0 if the entry cannot be found or its rarity is negative.

arguments.name: journalIdOrTypeId
arguments.description: Journal entry identifier or the ID of an item type, actor type, or actor class whose associated journal reward value is returned.

uses.description: Returns 1 if any of the player's tracked journal goal slots (goal0 through goal53) currently holds the specified goal string (compared after sanitization), otherwise 0.

arguments.name: goalId
arguments.description: Goal identifier compared (case-insensitive after sanitization) against the player's tracked goal slots.

uses.description: Returns 1 if any of the player's tracked journal goal slots (goal0 through goal53) currently holds the specified goal string AND its corresponding 'goalNDone' global is non-zero, otherwise 0.

arguments.name: goalId
arguments.description: Goal identifier compared (case-insensitive after sanitization) against the player's tracked goal slots; the matching 'Done' flag must also be set.

uses.description: Without the noteIndex argument, returns the index of the first journal note whose text matches the given content (or -1 if no match). With both arguments, returns 1 if the journal note at the given numeric index contains the specified text, otherwise 0.

arguments.name: noteIndexOrText
arguments.description: Either the note text to look for (one-argument form) or the numeric note index whose text is being compared (two-argument form).

arguments.name: text
arguments.description: Optional text to compare against the note at the given index. Required only in the two-argument form.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named action has the specified property matching the given value, otherwise 0. Used to test fields, list-membership, or enum flags on an Action data entry.

arguments.name: actionId
arguments.description: Identifier of an action in the data tables.

arguments.name: property
arguments.description: Name of a property/field on the Action to check.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Value to compare the property against; supports equality, list-contains, and enum flag checks via DataManager.

uses.description: Returns the numeric value of a named field on the specified action, parsed as a float. Useful for reading numeric properties on Action data entries.

arguments.name: actionId
arguments.description: Identifier of an action in the data tables.

arguments.name: property
arguments.description: Name of a numeric field on the Action to look up and parse as a float.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named support ability has the specified property matching the given value, otherwise 0. Used to test fields, list-membership, or enum flags on a SupportAbility data entry.

arguments.name: supportAbilityId
arguments.description: Identifier of a support ability in the data tables.

arguments.name: property
arguments.description: Name of a property/field on the SupportAbility to check.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Value to compare the property against; supports equality, list-contains, and enum flag checks via DataManager.

uses.description: Returns the numeric value of a named field on the specified support ability, parsed as a float. Useful for reading numeric properties on SupportAbility data entries.

arguments.name: supportAbilityId
arguments.description: Identifier of a support ability in the data tables.

arguments.name: property
arguments.description: Name of a numeric field on the SupportAbility to look up and parse as a float.

uses.description: Returns the base MP cost of the named action, evaluated with no caster, target, or rank context. Useful for previewing an action's nominal cost.

arguments.name: actionId
arguments.description: Identifier of an action whose mpCost formula is evaluated with default context.

uses.description: Returns the MP cost of the named action as it would apply to the caster (using the caster's rank in that action as the rank). Returns -1 if the caster is null or the action does not exist.

arguments.name: actionId
arguments.description: Identifier of an action whose mpCost formula is evaluated for the caster, including the caster's rank in that action.

uses.description: Returns the MP cost of the named action as it would apply to the target (using the target's rank in that action as the rank). Returns -1 if the target is null or the action does not exist.

arguments.name: actionId
arguments.description: Identifier of an action whose mpCost formula is evaluated for the target, including the target's rank in that action.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named control command is currently being held down, otherwise 0. Used for real-time input checks in formulas.

arguments.name: commandId
arguments.description: Identifier of a control command tracked by ControlsManager.

uses.description: Returns the duration (in seconds) that the named control command has currently been held down. Returns 0 if the command is not held.

arguments.name: commandId
arguments.description: Identifier of a control command tracked by ControlsManager.

uses.description: Returns the recent number of presses of the named control command (used for multi-tap input detection). Returns 0 if the command has not been tracked.

arguments.name: commandId
arguments.description: Identifier of a control command tracked by ControlsManager.

uses.description: Returns the time (in game-time units) since the named control command was last pressed. Useful for detecting freshness of an input.

arguments.name: commandId
arguments.description: Identifier of a control command tracked by ControlsManager.

uses.description: Returns the caster's timed-hit power multiplier (and queues visual/sound effects on the target) if the caster has the timed-hit ability AND the named control command was tapped within a narrow timing window during the caster's animation; otherwise returns 0. Used to implement input-timed crit/bonus mechanics.

arguments.name: commandId
arguments.description: Identifier of a control command whose press timing is being checked against the caster's animation phase.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the mouse cursor is currently over an item whose Item instance has the named property matching the specified value, otherwise 0. Used for input-driven tooltips and contextual UI checks.

arguments.name: property
arguments.description: Name of a property/field on the Item to check.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Value to compare the property against; supports equality, list-contains, and enum flag checks via DataManager.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the mouse cursor is currently over an item whose ItemType has the named property matching the specified value, otherwise 0. Like mousePointingAtItemWith but inspects the underlying item type rather than the instance.

arguments.name: property
arguments.description: Name of a property/field on the ItemType to check.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Value to compare the property against; supports equality, list-contains, and enum flag checks via DataManager.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the mouse cursor is currently over an Actor whose property matches the specified value, otherwise 0.

arguments.name: property
arguments.description: Name of a property/field on the Actor to check.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Value to compare the property against; supports equality, list-contains, and enum flag checks via DataManager.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the mouse cursor is currently over an Actor whose actorType has the named property matching the specified value, otherwise 0.

arguments.name: property
arguments.description: Name of a property/field on the actorType to check.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Value to compare the property against; supports equality, list-contains, and enum flag checks via DataManager.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the mouse cursor is over a tile whose terrainType has the named property matching the specified value, otherwise 0.

arguments.name: property
arguments.description: Name of a property/field on the TerrainType to check.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Value to compare the property against; supports equality, list-contains, and enum flag checks via DataManager.

uses.description: With three arguments (x, y, distance), returns 1 if the mouse cursor's tile coordinate is within the given tile distance of (x, y) and 0 otherwise. With two arguments, returns the actual tile distance from the cursor to (x, y), or -1 if the cursor is offscreen.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: X tile coordinate to measure distance from. Parsed as an integer.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: Y tile coordinate to measure distance from. Parsed as an integer.

arguments.name: maxDistance
arguments.description: Optional maximum tile distance. When provided, the operator returns 1 if the cursor is within this distance and 0 otherwise; when omitted, it returns the actual distance (or -1 if offscreen).

uses.description: With three arguments (x, y, distance), returns 1 if the mouse cursor's screen position is within the given pixel distance of (x, y) and 0 otherwise. With two arguments, returns the actual screen pixel distance, or -1 if the cursor is offscreen.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: X screen coordinate to measure distance from. Parsed as a float.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: Y screen coordinate to measure distance from. Parsed as a float.

arguments.name: maxDistance
arguments.description: Optional maximum pixel distance. When provided, the operator returns 1 if the cursor is within this distance and 0 otherwise; when omitted, it returns the actual distance (or -1 if offscreen).

uses.description: Returns 1 if a UI window with the specified name (or alias) is currently open and visible, otherwise 0. Useful for UI-state-dependent logic.

arguments.name: windowName
arguments.description: Name or alias of a UI window whose visibility is checked.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the mouse cursor is currently over a UI window whose name or alias matches the specified value, otherwise 0. Used for UI-driven contextual checks.

arguments.name: windowName
arguments.description: Name or alias of a UI window the cursor must be hovering over.

uses.description: Returns the X offset of the mouse cursor within the named UI window (cursor X minus window X). Returns -1 if the cursor is not over the named window.

arguments.name: windowName
arguments.description: Name or alias of a UI window whose local-X mouse coordinate is queried.

uses.description: Returns the Y offset of the mouse cursor within the named UI window (cursor Y minus window Y). Returns -1 if the cursor is not over the named window.

arguments.name: windowName
arguments.description: Name or alias of a UI window whose local-Y mouse coordinate is queried.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the mouse cursor's position within the named window is within the given pixel distance of an (x, y) point inside that window (otherwise 0). With only the window and point provided (no distance), returns the actual pixel distance, or -1 if the cursor is not over the named window.

arguments.name: windowName
arguments.description: Name or alias of a UI window whose local mouse coordinates are used.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: X coordinate inside the window to measure distance from. Parsed as a float.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: Y coordinate inside the window to measure distance from. Parsed as a float.

arguments.name: maxDistance
arguments.description: Optional maximum pixel distance. When provided, the operator returns 1 if the cursor is within this distance and 0 otherwise; when omitted, it returns the actual distance (or -1 if the cursor is not over the window).

uses.description: Returns 1 if the most recent ActorValueAffecter that landed a hit (Globals.previousAvAffecter) has the specified ID, otherwise 0. Used for chaining reactive logic to a specific recent effect.

arguments.name: affecterId
arguments.description: Identifier of an ActorValueAffecter to compare against the most recent landed affecter.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the most recent ActorValueAffecter that landed a hit was cast by the current caster AND has the specified ID, otherwise 0. A caster-restricted variant of thisPreviousAVHit.

arguments.name: affecterId
arguments.description: Identifier of an ActorValueAffecter to compare against the most recent landed affecter (only counted if the caster matches).

uses.description: Returns 1 if the named mod is installed (its folder exists) and enabled, otherwise 0. Lookup matches the mod ID directly first, then falls back to matching by 'name without special characters'.

arguments.name: modId
arguments.description: Identifier or sanitized name of a mod whose enabled+installed state is checked.

uses.description: Creates a transient clone of the caster and returns the specified actor value from the clone. Used to read the caster's stats without any side effects of buffs/effects that depend on identity tracking.

arguments.name: actorValue
arguments.description: Name of an actor value to read from the cloned caster.

uses.description: Returns the tile distance (Chebyshev-style, as used in tile-based movement) between two points. Three call forms are supported: with 2 arguments (x, y) the start is the caster's tile; with 3 arguments (actorId, x, y) the start is that actor's tile; with 4 arguments (x1, y1, x2, y2) both endpoints are explicit.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Either the X of the destination (two-arg form), the actor ID whose tile is the start (three-arg form), or the X of the start (four-arg form).

arguments.name: arg2
arguments.description: Either the Y of the destination (two-arg form), the X of the destination (three-arg form), or the Y of the start (four-arg form). Parsed as an integer.

arguments.name: arg3
arguments.description: Either the Y of the destination (three-arg form) or the X of the destination (four-arg form). Parsed as an integer; required for the three- and four-argument forms.

arguments.name: arg4
arguments.description: The Y of the destination in the four-argument form. Parsed as an integer.

uses.description: Returns the raw Euclidean distance between two points. Three call forms are supported: with 2 arguments (x, y) the start is the caster's tile; with 3 arguments (actorId, x, y) the start is that actor's tile; with 4 arguments (x1, y1, x2, y2) both endpoints are explicit.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.description: Either the X of the destination (two-arg form), the actor ID whose tile is the start (three-arg form), or the X of the start (four-arg form).

arguments.name: arg2
arguments.description: Either the Y of the destination (two-arg form), the X of the destination (three-arg form), or the Y of the start (four-arg form). Parsed as an integer.

arguments.name: arg3
arguments.description: Either the Y of the destination (three-arg form) or the X of the destination (four-arg form). Parsed as an integer; required for the three- and four-argument forms.

arguments.name: arg4
arguments.description: The Y of the destination in the four-argument form. Parsed as an integer.

uses.description: Returns 1 if there is a target actor for this evaluation, otherwise 0. Used to gate logic on whether an action has actually been targeted at someone.
Returns 1 if there is a target actor AND that target is a different actor instance from the caster, otherwise 0. Used to gate logic on having a distinct, non-self target.
Returns 1 if the target actor is the same as the zone's currently-active actor (the actor whose turn or action is being processed), otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the caster and target are the same actor (matched by ID), otherwise 0. Useful for distinguishing self-targeted actions.
Returns 1 if the caster and target are different actors (matched by ID), otherwise 0. The negation of m:casterIsTarget.
Returns 1 if the target actor's ID matches the player actor ID stored in the game state, otherwise 0. Useful for player-only logic.
Returns 1 if the target actor's faction is the 'player' faction, otherwise 0. A broader check than m:tIsPlayer that matches any actor on the player's side.
Returns the number of tile coordinates currently selected as targets for the action being set up. Includes all tiles regardless of whether an actor occupies them.
Returns the number of currently-selected target tiles that have an actor standing on them. A subset of m:numSelectedTargets restricted to tiles with actors present.
Returns 1 if the caster and the target actor share either their X tile coordinate or their Y tile coordinate (i.e., they are on the same orthogonal axis), otherwise 0.

Returns 1 if the caster and the target tile (targetTC) share either their X tile coordinate or their Y tile coordinate, otherwise 0. Like m:casterAndTargetSameXOrY but uses the explicit target tile rather than the target actor.
Returns the fraction of the caster's max HP that they have lost, in the range 0 (full HP) to 1 (zero HP). Useful for low-HP scaling effects.
Returns the fraction of the target's max HP that they have lost, in the range 0 (full HP) to 1 (zero HP). Useful for execute/finisher-style abilities that scale with target damage taken.

Returns 1 if the target's current HP fraction (HP / HPMax) is less than or equal to the parenthesized threshold, otherwise 0. The threshold is supplied via the parenthesized argument such as 'targetPercentHPLowerThan(0.5)'.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Fraction in [0, 1] supplied via a parenthesized argument; the target's HP fraction is compared against this value. Can also be a sub-formula evaluated in the same way.

uses.description: Returns 10% of the target's TrueHPMax, capped at 5. Useful for HP-based scaling capped to a manageable range.
Returns 1 if the target's current HP is strictly less than 11 (i.e., 10 or less), otherwise 0. Useful as a low-HP execute predicate.

Returns 90% of the target's current HP if that would leave the target with at least 2 HP afterward; otherwise returns 0. Used to compute a damage value that scales with target HP but is guaranteed not to kill.
Returns the most recent HP damage taken by the target actor (clamped at 0 so healing/negative damage reports 0). Used to reactively scale effects with the last damage incident.
Returns the most recent HP damage taken by the target actor, clamped at 0 and capped at the target's HP value just before that damage was applied. Useful for not over-counting overkill damage.
Returns the most recent HP damage taken by the caster actor (clamped at 0 so healing/negative damage reports 0). Useful for retaliation effects keyed to recent damage.
Returns the most recent HP damage taken by the caster actor, clamped at 0 and capped at the caster's HP value just before that damage was applied. Avoids over-counting overkill damage.
Returns the most recent healing received by the target, expressed as a non-positive value (Math.Min of 0 and the last damage event). For genuine damage events this returns 0.
Returns the caster's HP value just before the most recent damage event (clamped at 0). Useful for computing damage relative to pre-hit HP.
Returns the target's HP value just before the most recent damage event (clamped at 0). Useful for computing damage relative to pre-hit HP.
Returns 1 if the caster's most recent HP damage taken is strictly greater than the parenthesized value, otherwise 0.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Threshold supplied via a parenthesized argument; the caster's last damage is compared against this value. Can also be a sub-formula evaluated in the same way.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the caster's most recent HP damage taken is strictly less than the parenthesized value, otherwise 0.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Threshold supplied via a parenthesized argument; the caster's last damage is compared against this value. Can also be a sub-formula evaluated in the same way.

uses.description: Returns the minimum of the caster's most recent HP damage taken (clamped at 0) and the caster's Life skill value. Used for skill-bounded retaliation.
Returns the minimum of the caster's most recent HP damage taken (clamped at 0) and half the caster's Life skill (floored at 1). Used for skill-bounded retaliation with a smaller cap.
Returns 1 if the caster's most recent HP damage taken is strictly less than (floor of half the Life skill) + 1, otherwise 0. Used as a 'small damage' predicate based on the Life skill.
Returns 1 if the caster's most recent HP damage taken is strictly less than (floor of half the Life skill) + 2, otherwise 0. A slightly more generous version of the Plus1 variant.
Returns 1 if the caster's most recent HP damage taken is strictly less than (floor of half the Life skill) + 3, otherwise 0. A still more generous version of the Plus2 variant.
Returns 1 if the caster's MP equals exactly the parenthesized value, otherwise 0.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Numeric MP value supplied via a parenthesized argument; the caster's MP is compared against this. Can also be a sub-formula evaluated in the same way.

uses.description: Returns the MP cost of an ability the caster is currently casting or charging (the action queued for them in the zone's act timer). Returns 0 if the caster is not currently casting/charging anything.

Returns an evasion multiplier based on the facing angle between caster and target, rounded to the nearest 45 degrees and converted through the evasion lookup table (capped at 1). Returns 0 if either actor is missing or the target is incapacitated; returns 1 immediately if the target's actor type is directionless.
Same as m:evasionFacing, but with 0.1 subtracted from the result. Used when computing the evasion difference relative to a 'backstab' baseline.

Returns the difference between the evasion multiplier as currently facing and the evasion multiplier as if the target were facing the opposite direction. Used to quantify the evasion swing from flipping the target's facing.

Returns a flanking-bonus evasion modifier: a fraction of the target's PhysEva (scaled by the evasion multiplier at the current facing) capped at the target's PhysEvaFlank stat. Returns 0 if the caster or target is missing, the target is incapacitated, the target has no PhysEvaFlank, the target is directionless, or the facing difference is exactly 0.
Returns 1 if the facing angle between caster and target is within 45 degrees of directly forward (315°–360° or 0°–45°), otherwise 0. Directionless target types always return 0.
Returns 1 if the facing angle between caster and target is within 45 degrees of directly behind (135°–225°), otherwise 0. Directionless target types always return 0.
Returns the caster's facing rotation in degrees, snapped to the nearest 90° (or 45° if the caster's actor type uses 8-direction stances) and normalized into [0, 360).
Returns the target's facing rotation in degrees, snapped to the nearest 90° (or 45° if the target's actor type uses 8-direction stances) and normalized into [0, 360).
Returns 1 if the caster is hostile to the player faction, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the caster is hostile to the player faction, otherwise 0. Behaves identically to m:cHostile.
Returns 1 if the target is hostile to the player faction, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the caster and target share the same hostile flag (both hostile or both friendly), otherwise 0. Useful for ally/enemy detection.
Returns 1 if the caster and target have different hostile flags (one hostile and one friendly), otherwise 0. Useful for detecting cross-faction targets.
Returns 1 if the caster has a nonzero movement vector this frame, otherwise 0. Detects whether the caster is in motion.
Returns 1 if the caster is currently controllable by the player, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the caster's actor type is marked humanoid, otherwise 0. Useful for humanoid-only ability prerequisites.
Returns 1 if the target's actor type is marked humanoid, otherwise 0. Useful for humanoid-only ability prerequisites.
Returns 1 if the target is flagged as unkillable, otherwise 0. Used to gate damage logic that should respect immortality.
Returns 1 if the target's actor type is tameable, the caster and target are on opposite hostile sides, and the target is not incapacitated; otherwise 0. Used for taming-action eligibility checks.
Returns 1 if the target's actor type is tameable, otherwise 0. A relaxed version of m:tTameable that does not require hostile-side mismatch or unconsciousness.
Returns 1 if the target is currently controllable by the player, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the caster and target share the same controllable flag (both controllable or both not), otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the target is flagged as fearless (cannot be affected by fear/morale-style effects), otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the target's actor type is marked unrevivable (resurrection abilities cannot bring them back), otherwise 0.
Returns the target's intelligence rating (used by AI to gate available behaviors).
Returns the weight value configured on the target's actor type. Useful for weight-dependent mechanics (knockback, sinking, etc.).
Returns the caster's AI priority multiplier (used by enemy AI to weight which target/action to prefer).
Returns the target's AI priority multiplier (used by enemy AI to weight which target/action to prefer).
Returns the inventory slot index of the target's favored (main) hand. Useful for index-based weapon lookups.
Returns 1 if the target's favored hand is slot 0 (meaning the offhand is slot 1, the conventional layout), otherwise 0.

Returns 1 if the zone's currently-active actor is allowed to act this turn, otherwise 0. Considers global zone restrictions (cantAct flag, peaceful zones with cannotActInPeacefulZones items, tiles with cannotAct items) in addition to the actor's input permissions.
Returns 1 if the zone's currently-active actor is allowed to move this turn, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the zone's currently-active actor is allowed to manipulate items this turn, otherwise 0.
Returns the caster's highest ranged combat skill, picked from skill_Bow and skill_Aim.
Returns the caster's lowest melee weapon skill, picked from skill_Sword, skill_Axe, skill_Hammer, skill_Spear, and skill_Flail (capped at 99 if none are set).
Returns the caster's lowest close-combat melee skill, picked from skill_Fist and skill_Dagger (capped at 99 if none are set).
Returns the caster's lowest magic skill, picked from skill_Fire, skill_Ice, skill_Elec, skill_Wind, skill_Life, and skill_Ruin (capped at 99 if none are set).
Returns 1 if the caster and target share at least one actor class (across any of their four class slots), OR if they share the same actor type, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the caster and target share at least one actor class (across any of their four class slots), otherwise 0. Like m:anySameClassesOrActorType but does not consider actor type as a fallback.
Returns 1 if the caster and target share the same actorTypeID, otherwise 0.
Counts how many selectable, non-'dummy' actor classes the caster currently meets the requirements for. Useful for class-selection UI logic.
Returns the total number of selectable, non-'dummy' actor classes defined in the data tables. Useful for displaying out-of-N class completion.
Returns the number of actor classes the player has discovered/unlocked (excluding any starting with 'dummy').
Returns 1 if the current zone is in combat mode, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the current zone is NOT in combat mode, otherwise 0. The negation of m:combat.
Returns 1 if it is currently the caster's turn in combat, OR if combat is not active (so the caster can act freely), otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if combat is active and it is NOT currently the caster's turn, otherwise 0. Useful for off-turn reactive checks.
Returns 1 if the action currently being processed is an item-manipulation action, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the current turn's actor has not yet acted (OR if combat is not active), otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the current turn's actor has already acted, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the previous combat turn's actor did not act on their turn, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the current turn's actor has already moved this turn, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the current turn's actor has not yet moved (OR if combat is not active), otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the previous combat turn's actor did not move on their turn, otherwise 0.
Returns the target's act timer value: the total timer if the target has an active timer, otherwise the target's actTime actor value (a default).
Returns the time remaining on the target's act timer if they have one, otherwise the target's actTime actor value as a fallback.
Returns 1 if the target's act timer's remaining time is strictly less than the parenthesized value, otherwise 0. Used to check whether the target can act before a given time elapses.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Time-remaining threshold supplied via a parenthesized argument; the target's timer is compared against this value. Can also be a sub-formula evaluated in the same way.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the target is incapacitated OR has 0 or fewer HP, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the target is currently casting or charging an ability (i.e., interruptible), otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the caster is in critical condition (as determined by the actor's isCritical method, typically low HP), otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the target is in critical condition (as determined by the actor's isCritical method, typically low HP), otherwise 0.

Returns 1 if the caster has at least one currently-active harmful status that is eligible for non-specific cure removal (with normal timed or persistent duration), otherwise 0. The 'wet' status is excluded if the caster is currently drowning.

Returns 1 if the target has at least one currently-active harmful status that is eligible for non-specific cure removal (with normal timed or persistent duration), otherwise 0. The 'wet' status is excluded if the target is currently drowning.

Counts how many currently-active actor value effects on the target are harmful, non-world-time, eligible for non-specific removal, with normal timed or persistent duration. Useful for status-purge scaling.

Counts how many currently-active actor value effects on the target are non-world-time and eligible for non-specific removal, with normal timed or persistent duration (regardless of whether they are harmful or beneficial).
Returns the time remaining on the target's currently-active casting/charging act timer. Returns 0 if the target is not currently casting or charging.
Returns the caster's morale minus the target's morale, clamped to 0 if negative. Used for morale-driven combat advantage calculations.

Returns the tile distance between the caster and the target (or targetTC if no target actor is set). If a parenthesized threshold is supplied, returns 1 if the distance is greater than or equal to that threshold, otherwise 0.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Optional distance threshold supplied via a parenthesized argument; when nonzero, the operator returns 1 if the actual distance meets or exceeds it, otherwise 0. When omitted (or 0), the actual distance is returned.

uses.description: Returns the tile distance between the player fleet's flagship and the target actor. Returns 0 if the player fleet or flagship cannot be found.
Returns the raw Euclidean distance between the caster and the target. Unlike m:tileDistance, this is not snapped to tile-based movement distance.
Returns the globally-cached tile distance from the caster (used during cast-iteration loops). Reflects whatever value Globals.tilesDistanceFromCaster currently holds.

Returns the tile distance the caster has traveled since beginning their current movement (the difference between their starting position and current position). Returns 0 if the caster has not started moving.
Returns the total number of tiles moved by the current actor during this turn.
Returns the tile distance from the caster's original (pre-movement) position to the target. Falls back to the distance from the caster's current position if no movement has been recorded.
Counts how many non-incapacitated actors are within the parenthesized tile radius of the caster. The radius is supplied via a parenthesized argument such as 'numActorsWithin(3)'.

arguments.name: radius
arguments.description: Tile radius supplied via a parenthesized argument; the search around the caster is performed within this radius. Can also be a sub-formula evaluated in the same way.

uses.description: Counts how many non-incapacitated actors of the opposite hostile side as the caster are within the parenthesized tile radius. Used for measuring local threat density.

arguments.name: radius
arguments.description: Tile radius supplied via a parenthesized argument; the search around the caster is performed within this radius. Can also be a sub-formula evaluated in the same way.

uses.description: Counts how many non-incapacitated actors of the opposite hostile side as the caster are within 1 tile of the caster (immediately adjacent or co-located). A fixed-radius variant of m:numEnemiesWithin.
Counts how many non-incapacitated actors share both the caster's hostile flag AND the caster's faction, within the parenthesized tile radius. A stricter ally count than m:numAlliesWithin.

arguments.name: radius
arguments.description: Tile radius supplied via a parenthesized argument; the search around the caster is performed within this radius. Can also be a sub-formula evaluated in the same way.

uses.description: Counts how many non-incapacitated actors share the caster's hostile flag within the parenthesized tile radius. Used for measuring local allied density.

arguments.name: radius
arguments.description: Tile radius supplied via a parenthesized argument; the search around the caster is performed within this radius. Can also be a sub-formula evaluated in the same way.

uses.description: Counts how many non-incapacitated actors share the caster's hostile flag within the parenthesized tile radius of the target's tile (or targetTC if no target actor is set).

arguments.name: radius
arguments.description: Tile radius supplied via a parenthesized argument; the search around the target is performed within this radius.

uses.description: Counts how many non-incapacitated allies of the caster (sharing hostile flag) are within the parenthesized tile radius of the target's tile, excluding the caster themselves.

arguments.name: radius
arguments.description: Tile radius supplied via a parenthesized argument; the search around the target is performed within this radius.

uses.description: Counts how many non-incapacitated allies of the caster (sharing hostile flag) within the parenthesized tile radius of the target's tile are positioned on the same row or column as the target.

arguments.name: radius
arguments.description: Tile radius supplied via a parenthesized argument; the search around the target is performed within this radius.

uses.description: Counts how many non-incapacitated allies of the caster within the parenthesized tile radius of the target's tile both meet melee weapon-element requirements AND can reach the target with their weapon. Used for measuring how surrounded a target is by melee threats.

arguments.name: radius
arguments.description: Tile radius supplied via a parenthesized argument; the search around the target is performed within this radius.

uses.description: Counts how many non-incapacitated allies of the caster (excluding the caster themselves) within the parenthesized tile radius of the target's tile can melee attack the target.

arguments.name: radius
arguments.description: Tile radius supplied via a parenthesized argument; the search around the target is performed within this radius.

uses.description: Counts how many non-incapacitated allies of the caster within the parenthesized tile radius of the target's tile both meet ranged weapon-element requirements AND can reach the target with their weapon.

arguments.name: radius
arguments.description: Tile radius supplied via a parenthesized argument; the search around the target is performed within this radius.

uses.description: Counts how many actors are within the parenthesized tile radius of the target's tile, excluding the caster themselves. Unlike the 'allies' variants, this counts actors of any hostile flag.

arguments.name: radius
arguments.description: Tile radius supplied via a parenthesized argument; the search around the target is performed within this radius.

uses.description: Counts how many non-incapacitated actors of the opposite hostile side as the caster are within the parenthesized tile radius of the target's tile.

arguments.name: radius
arguments.description: Tile radius supplied via a parenthesized argument; the search around the target is performed within this radius.

uses.description: Counts how many non-incapacitated actors lie on the straight line between the caster's tile and the target's tile (exclusive of both endpoints). Useful for piercing/line-of-fire abilities.

Counts how many non-incapacitated enemies (opposite hostile flag) within the parenthesized tile radius of the target are NOT already on the player's selected-targets list and are NOT the current target. Useful for highlighting still-pickable enemies for multi-target abilities.

arguments.name: radius
arguments.description: Tile radius supplied via a parenthesized argument; the search around the target is performed within this radius.

uses.description: Returns the number of actors standing on the target tile (or targetTC). Useful for checking stacking on a single tile.
Returns 1 if more than one actor is standing on the target tile (or targetTC), otherwise 0.
Counts how many non-controllable actors (NPCs) are standing on the target tile (or targetTC).
Counts how many actors standing on the target tile (or targetTC) currently have a nonzero movement vector this frame.
Counts how many green tile-highlight markers (used by the UI to indicate valid targets) are within 1 tile of the caster. Useful for AI/UI scoring of nearby move options.
Returns 1 if the target tile (targetTC) blocks line-of-effect (terrain or items that interrupt area-of-effect propagation), otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the tile one step toward the caster from the target tile is NOT occupiable (i.e., something obstructs the move-to-target path one tile short), otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the target tile (targetTC) is itself currently occupiable (using the 'oneTileWalk_selectActors' AoE template), otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the tile directly behind the target (the tile farther from the caster, along the caster→target direction) is occupiable, otherwise 0. Used for push/knock-back validity.
Returns 1 if the tile directly behind the target (the tile farther from the caster) does NOT block line-of-effect, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the caster has unobstructed line-of-sight AND line-of-effect from their tile to the target tile (with airborne flag enabled), otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the tile one step closer to the caster than the target (the would-be landing tile for moving to the target) is currently occupied by an actor other than the caster, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the tile one step beyond the target (the would-be landing tile for moving past the target) is currently occupied by an actor other than the caster, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the would-be landing tile for moving to the target either is occupied by another actor OR is not occupiable, otherwise 0. A combined obstacle check.
Returns 1 if the would-be landing tile for moving past the target either is occupied by another actor OR is not occupiable, otherwise 0. A combined obstacle check for move-past actions.
Returns 1 if there is any actor (other than the caster) standing on the tile directly behind the target (along the caster→target direction, allowing diagonals), otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if there is an ally of the caster (sharing hostile flag, excluding the caster) standing on the tile directly behind the target, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the target has at least one weapon that could be broken right now (across both hands), otherwise 0. Determined by simulating the break-weapon code with actuallyBreakIt=false.
Returns 1 if the target's main-hand weapon is present, not already broken, and not flagged as unbreakable, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the target's offhand weapon (the slot opposite to favoredHand) is present, not already broken, and not flagged as unbreakable, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the caster's currently-equipped weapon is broken, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the target has any broken weapon in their hand slots (slot 0 or slot 1), otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the target's main-hand weapon is broken, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the target's offhand weapon (the slot opposite to favoredHand) is broken, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the target's armor could be broken right now, otherwise 0. Determined by simulating the break-armor code with actuallyBreakIt=false.
Returns 1 if the target's armor (inventory slot 2) is currently broken, otherwise 0.
Counts how many items in the caster's inventory (top-level, not searching containers) are currently broken.
Counts how many items in the target's inventory (top-level, not searching containers) are currently broken.
Counts how many items in the player's stash inventory are currently broken.
Returns 1 if the target has at least one item that could be dropped right now, otherwise 0. Determined by simulating the dropItem code with actuallyDropIt=false.
Returns 1 if the target has any weapon equipped (in slot 0 or slot 1), otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the target has armor equipped (in slot 2), otherwise 0.
Returns the quantity of gold pieces (item ID 'gp') in the target's inventory.
Returns the total sale value of the target's entire inventory.
Returns the floored bribe cost for the target's combat group, computed as 14% of the group's power level.

Counts how many augment items are inside the target's main-hand weapon's container that have weapon-applicable reactions or effects. Returns 0 if the target has no main-hand weapon or it has no container.

Counts how many augment items are inside the target's offhand weapon's container that have weapon-applicable reactions or effects. Returns 0 if the target has no offhand weapon or it has no container.
Counts how many augment items are inside the target's armor container (slot 2) that have armor-applicable reactions or effects. Returns 0 if the target has no armor or it has no container.
Counts how many items the caster has equipped (in weapon slots 0–1, armor slot 2, or accessory slots 3–4) whose ItemType belongs to any non-empty exclusive-equipment category.
Counts how many items the target has equipped (in weapon slots 0–1, armor slot 2, or accessory slots 3–4) whose ItemType belongs to any non-empty exclusive-equipment category.
Returns the player's current money. If a parenthesized threshold is supplied, returns 1 if the player has at least that much money and 0 otherwise.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Optional threshold supplied via a parenthesized argument; when positive, the operator returns 1 if the player's money is greater than or equal to it. When omitted (or non-positive), the actual money value is returned.

uses.description: Returns the current party size. If a parenthesized threshold is supplied, returns 1 if the party size meets or exceeds the threshold and 0 otherwise.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Optional threshold supplied via a parenthesized argument; when positive, the operator returns 1 if the party size meets or exceeds it. When omitted (or non-positive), the actual party size is returned.

uses.description: Returns the current landing party's size. If a parenthesized threshold is supplied, returns 1 if the size meets or exceeds the threshold and 0 otherwise.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Optional threshold supplied via a parenthesized argument; when positive, the operator returns 1 if the landing party size meets or exceeds it. When omitted, the actual size is returned.

uses.description: Returns the combined count of barracks party members and active party members. If a parenthesized threshold is supplied, returns 1 if the combined size meets or exceeds the threshold and 0 otherwise.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Optional threshold supplied via a parenthesized argument; when positive, the operator returns 1 if the combined barracks+party size meets or exceeds it. When omitted, the actual combined size is returned.

uses.description: Returns the player's combined fame (the sum of explore, trade, and combat fame globals).
Returns the fame requirement for the player's next royal quest tier.
Returns 1 if the player's highest single-track fame (the max of explore, trade, and combat) meets the next royal quest fame requirement, otherwise 0.

Returns the player's favor with their own faction (the faction stored in the 'playerFaction' global). If a parenthesized threshold is supplied, returns 1 if the favor meets or exceeds the threshold and 0 otherwise.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Optional threshold supplied via a parenthesized argument; when positive, the operator returns 1 if the favor meets or exceeds it. When omitted, the actual favor value is returned.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the player's favor with their own faction is strictly less than the parenthesized threshold, otherwise 0.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Threshold supplied via a parenthesized argument; the player's own-faction favor must be strictly less than this for the operator to return 1.

uses.description: Returns the player's favor with the target's faction. If no target actor is set, falls back to the current conversation partner.
Returns 1 if the player's favor with the target's faction is greater than or equal to the parenthesized threshold, otherwise 0. Falls back to the current conversation partner if no target is set.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Threshold supplied via a parenthesized argument; the player's favor with the target's faction must meet or exceed this for the operator to return 1.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the player's favor with the target's faction is greater than or equal to the negation of the parenthesized threshold (i.e., favor ≥ -threshold), otherwise 0. Useful for 'not too unfavorable' checks. Falls back to the current conversation partner if no target is set.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Threshold supplied via a parenthesized argument; the player's favor with the target's faction must be ≥ -threshold for the operator to return 1.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the player's favor with the target's faction is strictly less than the parenthesized threshold, otherwise 0. Falls back to the current conversation partner if no target is set.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Threshold supplied via a parenthesized argument; the player's favor with the target's faction must be strictly less than this for the operator to return 1.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the player's favor with the target's faction is strictly less than the negation of the parenthesized threshold (i.e., favor < -threshold), otherwise 0. Useful for 'very unfavorable' checks. Falls back to the current conversation partner if no target is set.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Threshold supplied via a parenthesized argument; the player's favor with the target's faction must be strictly less than -threshold for the operator to return 1.

uses.description: Returns the player party's current power level, including current HP and active state. Used for fair-fight scaling.
Returns the player party's base power level (without weighting by current HP/state). Used for stable scaling that doesn't fluctuate during combat.
Returns the total power level of all hostile actors currently in the zone, plus any power level reserved for actors still being spawned.
Returns the ratio of party base power level to total enemy power level (including pending spawns). Returns 0 if there are no enemies.
Counts how many discovered journal entries the player has whose rarity is not -1 (i.e., 'real' entries excluding placeholders).
Returns the number of journal entries the player has already sold/reported to vendors.
Counts how many of the player's tracked journal goal slots (goal0 through goal53) currently hold an active, non-completed goal.
Counts how many of the player's tracked journal goal slots (goal0 through goal53) hold a goal that has been marked completed.
Counts how many non-empty player-authored notes are present in the journal notes UI.
Returns the total journal-reward value of items the player has placed on museum tiles in the current zone. For each museum tile, the most valuable single item's journal reward is counted.

Counts how many discovered journal entries the player has that are not in the 'help' category, are unreported (not yet sold), and have rarity strictly greater than 2. If a parenthesized threshold is supplied, returns 1 if the count meets or exceeds it and 0 otherwise.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Optional threshold supplied via a parenthesized argument; when nonzero, the operator returns 1 if the count meets or exceeds it and 0 otherwise. When omitted, the actual count is returned.

uses.description: Counts how many actor types the player has killed whose journal entry exists but is not yet in the player's known journal entries list. Useful for tracking 'undiscovered after first kill' creatures.
Returns the player's currently-used cargo volume.
Returns the player's maximum cargo capacity.
Returns the player's remaining cargo space. If a parenthesized threshold is supplied, returns 1 if the remaining space meets or exceeds the threshold and 0 otherwise.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Optional threshold supplied via a parenthesized argument; when nonzero, the operator returns 1 if remaining cargo space meets or exceeds it. When omitted, the actual remaining space is returned.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the player's current cargo exceeds the maximum cargo capacity (overloaded), otherwise 0.
Returns the maximum amount of 'food' items the player could currently buy, constrained by both their available money (at the current food buy price) and their remaining cargo space.
Returns the total amount of HP repairs needed across the player's fleet.
Returns the cost in gold to fully repair the player's fleet at the current location, with the favor-based discount applied.
Returns the base (undiscounted) cost in gold to fully repair the player's fleet, without any favor-based discount applied.
Returns 1 if the player has enough money to buy the parenthesized quantity of food (at food's base value), otherwise 0.

arguments.name: quantity
arguments.description: Quantity of food supplied via a parenthesized argument; the operator checks the player has money to buy this many units of food.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the player has enough money to buy the parenthesized quantity of the current zone's location's trade-good item, otherwise 0.

arguments.name: quantity
arguments.description: Quantity of the trade good supplied via a parenthesized argument; the operator checks the player has money to buy this many units of the current location's trade good.
