uses.description: Returns 1 if any boat in the player's fleet has two or more equipped items of the same plain (non-reactive, non-effective) type, otherwise 0. Used to warn about duplicate-cannon arrangements.

Returns 1 if every boat in the player's fleet has no equipped items AND the cargo inventory contains at least one weapon-category item, otherwise 0. Used to alert that cannons are available but unequipped.
Returns 1 if the caster (a ship actor) is player-controllable OR has at least one non-incapacitated crew member among the first 5, otherwise 0. Used to gate boarding actions.
Returns 1 if the caster's actor type is flagged as a boat, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the target's actor type is flagged as a boat, otherwise 0.
Returns the configured value of the actor type of the boat at the given fleet index (supplied via the parenthesized argument). Returns 0 if the index is out of range.

arguments.name: index
arguments.description: Zero-based index into the player fleet's boats list supplied via a parenthesized argument.

uses.description: Returns the amount of food the player's fleet consumes per day.
Returns the total crew count across the player's fleet. If a parenthesized threshold is supplied, returns 1 if the size meets or exceeds the threshold and 0 otherwise.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Optional threshold supplied via a parenthesized argument; when positive, the operator returns 1 if the total crew size meets or exceeds it. When omitted, the actual size is returned.

uses.description: Returns the number of crew the player's fleet has (using the fleet's getNumCrew method, which may apply different counting rules than crewSize).
Returns the crew count of the boat at the given fleet index (supplied via the parenthesized argument). Returns 0 if the index is out of range.

arguments.name: index
arguments.description: Zero-based index into the player fleet's boats list supplied via a parenthesized argument.

uses.description: Returns the minimum-crew requirement from the caster's actor type (used to detect when a ship is undermanned).
Returns the caster's current crew count via the actor's getHowManyCrew method.
Returns the smaller of the caster's crew count and the parenthesized cap. Used to compute crew-scaled values that saturate at a fixed maximum.

arguments.name: cap
arguments.description: Maximum value supplied via a parenthesized argument; the operator returns Min(crewCount, cap).

uses.description: Returns a multiplier reflecting how undermanned the caster is: 1 by default, 0.75 if the caster's actor type requires more than 1 crew but currently has 1 or fewer, or 0.5 if it has half a crew member or fewer. Used for low-crew penalty scaling.
Returns the target's current crew count via the actor's getHowManyCrew method.
Returns the caster's crew morale as a fraction (player crew morale / 100) if the caster is player-controllable, otherwise returns a fixed 0.7. Used for ship abilities that scale with crew morale.
Returns the player's crew morale as a fraction (player crew morale / 100).
Returns 1 if the caster has at least one non-incapacitated crew member, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the caster has zero non-incapacitated crew members, otherwise 0.
Returns the player's raw crew morale value (0–100 scale).
Returns the number of boats in the player's fleet. If a parenthesized threshold is supplied, returns 1 if the size is strictly greater than the threshold and 0 otherwise.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Optional threshold supplied via a parenthesized argument; when positive, the operator returns 1 if the fleet size is strictly greater than it. When omitted, the actual fleet size is returned.

uses.description: Returns the number of boats in the target actor's fleet (using getBoats which may exclude inactive ships). Returns 0 if the target is not in a fleet.
Returns the raw boat-list size of the target actor's fleet (counting every entry, including any that getBoats might filter). Returns 0 if the target is not in a fleet.
Returns the number of items currently in the cargo of the target actor's fleet. Returns 0 if the target is not in a fleet or the fleet has no cargo.

Returns 1 if the player's fleet power level exceeds three times the bravery-scaled power level of the fleet of the actor the player is currently talking to, otherwise 0. Used by intimidate/flee dialogue logic.
Returns 1 if the player's fleet power level exceeds three times the bravery-scaled power level of the caster's fleet, otherwise 0. Used by AI/encounter logic to detect overwhelming player advantage.
Returns the player actor's current movement speed (scaled by 4) while moving. Returns 0 if the player is currently idle and not receiving movement input.
Returns 1 if the target's fleet is marked as a merchant fleet, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the target's fleet is marked as elite, otherwise 0.
Returns the total weight on the target tile (targetTC), summing the weights of all actors standing on it and all items located on it.
Counts how many buried items are on the target tile (targetTC), excluding items flagged as alwaysBuried (which don't count toward burial-state mechanics).
Returns 1 if the target tile (targetTC) contains any roof-class items, otherwise 0. Used to detect indoor vs outdoor positioning.
Returns the red channel (0–255) of the current zone's configured darkness color.
Returns the green channel (0–255) of the current zone's configured darkness color.
Returns the blue channel (0–255) of the current zone's configured darkness color.
Returns 1 if the zone is dark, its overall darkness is significant (R+G+B < 200), and the target tile is currently in shadow (not lit by any nearby light source), otherwise 0.
Returns the red channel (0–255) of the lighting color at the target tile. If the zone is not dark, this is the white color (255); otherwise the local lighting calculation is used.
Returns the green channel (0–255) of the lighting color at the target tile. If the zone is not dark, this is the white color (255); otherwise the local lighting calculation is used.
Returns the blue channel (0–255) of the lighting color at the target tile. If the zone is not dark, this is the white color (255); otherwise the local lighting calculation is used.
Returns the sum of the R, G, and B channels of the lighting color at the target tile (0–765). Useful as a scalar brightness metric.
Returns the percentage of zone darkness at the target tile relative to the zone's maximum darkness (0–100, rounded to 2 decimals). Returns 0 if the zone is not dark.
Returns the percentage of zone lightness at the target tile (100 minus the darkLevel percentage, rounded to 2 decimals). Returns 0 if the zone is not dark.
Returns the height/elevation of the target tile (target's tile or targetTC). Useful for terrain-elevation-based effects.
Returns 1 if the target tile (targetTC) is still in fog of war (not yet explored by the player in this zone), otherwise 0.
Returns the percentage of the current zone the player has explored, as a number from 0 to 100 (rounded to two decimals). Computed from the fog-of-war texture.
Returns 1 if the current zone is the overworld, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the current zone is flagged peaceful (combat restricted), otherwise 0.
Returns the current weather as a numeric enum value (cast from the game state's currentWeather field).
Returns the current wind rotation in degrees (the game state's windRot value).
Returns the width (size in X) of the current zone.
Returns the height (size in Y) of the current zone.
Returns the number of reactions defined on the terrain type at the target tile (target's tile or targetTC). Useful for measuring how reactive a tile's terrain is.
Returns 1 if the player's current location is flagged as a port. When the player is on the overworld with no current location, falls back to the nearest location within 4 tiles.

Returns the wealth level of the player's current location. When on the overworld with no current location, falls back to the nearest port within 4 tiles. If a parenthesized threshold is supplied, returns 1 if wealth meets or exceeds it and 0 otherwise.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Optional threshold supplied via a parenthesized argument; when positive, the operator returns 1 if the location's wealthLevel meets or exceeds it. When omitted, the actual wealth level is returned.

uses.description: Returns the player's favor with the faction that owns the player's current location, clamped at a minimum of -99. When on the overworld with no current location, falls back to the nearest port within 4 tiles. If a parenthesized threshold is supplied, returns 1 if favor meets or exceeds it and 0 otherwise.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Optional threshold supplied via a parenthesized argument; when positive, the operator returns 1 if the player's favor with the local faction meets or exceeds it. When omitted, the actual favor value is returned.

uses.description: Returns the per-day restock count of trade goods at the player's current location, computed from its wealth level and the value of its trade good's item type. The value is clamped to at least 1 and at least 2 if the location is at maximum wealth.
Returns the per-unit buy price of the cafe food at the player's current location.

Returns the total cost of buying enough cafe food at the player's current location to feed the player's full fleet crew. Equivalent to the per-unit food price multiplied by the player fleet's crew count.
Returns 1 if the player has enough money to feed the entire fleet crew at the current location's cafe, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the player's current location has at least one unlockable entry whose ID matches an actor type (i.e., a ship is unlockable here), otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the player's current location's X overworld coordinate is strictly less than the parenthesized threshold, otherwise 0.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Threshold supplied via a parenthesized argument; the location's X must be strictly less than this for the operator to return 1.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the player's current location's Y overworld coordinate is strictly less than the parenthesized threshold, otherwise 0.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Threshold supplied via a parenthesized argument; the location's Y must be strictly less than this for the operator to return 1.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the player's current location's X overworld coordinate is strictly greater than the parenthesized threshold, otherwise 0.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Threshold supplied via a parenthesized argument; the location's X must be strictly greater than this for the operator to return 1.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the player's current location's Y overworld coordinate is strictly greater than the parenthesized threshold, otherwise 0.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Threshold supplied via a parenthesized argument; the location's Y must be strictly greater than this for the operator to return 1.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the player's current location permits changing factions, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the current world day-of-the-week matches the day at the parenthesized index, otherwise 0.

arguments.name: dayIndex
arguments.description: Zero-based index into the configured days-of-the-week list, supplied via a parenthesized argument.

uses.description: Returns 1 if it is currently daytime in the world, otherwise 0.
Returns the world time in hours (a continuous floating-point world-clock value).
Returns the current day-of-month (1–30, floored). If a parenthesized threshold is supplied, returns 1 if the day meets or exceeds it and 0 otherwise.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Optional threshold supplied via a parenthesized argument; when positive, the operator returns 1 if the current day-of-month meets or exceeds it. When omitted, the actual day is returned.

uses.description: Returns the current calendar month as a floored numeric value (computed from worldTime_days).
Returns the total elapsed world days since the start of the game (1-indexed).
Returns the player's total accumulated gameplay time in seconds.
Returns the total real-time seconds elapsed since the game was launched (Game1.totalGameTime). Used by UI/animation logic.
Returns 1 if the most recent ActorValueAffecter evaluation registered a hit, otherwise 0. Useful for chaining effects to a successful prior application.
Returns 1 if the most recent ActorValueAffecter's random chance check rolled true (regardless of whether the affecter ultimately hit), otherwise 0.

Returns the overflow bonus from a hit-chance over 100% (excess chance / 100, minus 1, capped at 0.3, floored at 0). When the current ActorValueAffecter has a chance formula greater than 100%, the surplus contributes to a damage bonus.
Returns a random integer in the inclusive range [0, max] where max is the parenthesized argument (clamped to at least 1). Uses Game1.random as the source.

arguments.name: max
arguments.description: Maximum value of the rolled integer (inclusive), supplied via a parenthesized argument. Clamped to at least 1.

uses.description: Returns either 1 or -1 with equal probability, drawn from Game1.random.

Returns a deterministic 'random' integer in [0, max] (inclusive) seeded from the target's hash code, where max is supplied via the parenthesized argument (clamped to at least 1). Useful for stable per-target random values.

arguments.name: max
arguments.description: Maximum value of the rolled integer (inclusive), supplied via a parenthesized argument. Clamped to at least 1.

uses.description: Returns 1 if the extra-races content flag is enabled in the global game settings, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the global 'ruin mode' setting is enabled, otherwise 0.
Returns the total number of items currently being held by the player's cursor.
Returns 1 if the player's cursor is currently dragging an actor, otherwise 0.
Returns the elapsed time the player has been holding the current cursor-held item (used for input-driven mechanics).
Returns the elapsed time since the player last dropped an item from the cursor.
Returns 1 if the player is currently using a gamepad as the primary input (or last used one), otherwise 0.
Returns the X tile coordinate of the current action's task target (Globals.currentActionTaskTC.X). Useful inside action effects that should reference the action's target tile.
Returns the Y tile coordinate of the current action's task target (Globals.currentActionTaskTC.Y).
Returns 1 if the current zone is in cutscene mode (player input restricted while a script plays out), otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the player is currently allowed to save the game, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the player actor is currently allowed to act (no UI/cutscene/input lock), otherwise 0.
Returns the maximum party size the player can currently field.
Returns the current camera zoom scale.
Returns the mouse cursor's X screen-coordinate. Returns -1 when the cursor is offscreen (X < 0).
Returns the mouse cursor's Y screen-coordinate. Returns -1 when the cursor is offscreen (Y < 0).
Returns the X tile coordinate the mouse cursor is currently hovering over. Returns -1 when the cursor is offscreen.
Returns the Y tile coordinate the mouse cursor is currently hovering over. Returns -1 when the cursor is offscreen.
Returns the X delta of the mouse cursor's movement this frame.
Returns the Y delta of the mouse cursor's movement this frame.
Returns the change in the mouse scroll wheel this frame.
Returns 1 if debug mode is active, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the game is running in fullscreen mode, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if borderless-fullscreen is the active fullscreen mode, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the 'intense visual effects' option is enabled, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the colorblind-adjustments option is enabled, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the dyslexic-font option is enabled, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the retro-visuals option is enabled, otherwise 0.
Returns the player's master audio volume setting.
Returns the player's sound-effects volume setting.
Returns the player's music volume setting.
Returns the combat difficulty tier as an integer (1 for easiest at enemyStatsMultiplier 0.8, 2 for normal at 1.0, 3 at 1.2, 4 at 1.6, 5 at 1.8). Returns 0 for any other multiplier value.
Returns the combat AI difficulty/speed tier as an integer (1 at AIMaxTimePerTurnSpeedupMod -3, 2 at 0, 3 at 4, 4 at 10). Returns 0 for any other mod value.
Returns the global travel difficulty setting (survival-mode tuning).
Returns the global enemy stats multiplier (combat difficulty scaler).
Returns the global enemy spawn power modifier (used to scale spawn power levels in encounters).
Returns the player's bonus movement setting (a global accessibility/ease tuning value).
Returns the global AI-max-time-per-turn speed-up modifier (controls how fast enemy AI turns play out).
Returns the global AI plan rating threshold modifier (affects how aggressive enemy AI is in pursuing higher-rated plans).
Returns 1 if the player is in 'port-only save' mode (Globals.canSaveOutsidePort is false), otherwise 0.
Returns the player's XP multiplier setting (a global tuning value scaling XP gain).
Returns 1 if the timed-hits gameplay mode is enabled, otherwise 0.
Returns the configured multi-press maximum time (the threshold within which sequential clicks count as a single multi-press).
Returns the configured camera speed during combat.
Returns the configured dialog speed multiplier.
Returns 1 if keyboard controls are prioritized over mouse for menu navigation, otherwise 0.
Returns 1 if the game is configured to pause when the window loses focus, otherwise 0.
Returns the configured cursor display scale.
Returns the configured tooltip display scale.
